programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras (The Royals Next Door)
programme.desc: There's distress between three generations on Mother's Day, but it's fortunate that Stella is close by to reconcile matters

programme.title: Uaine
programme.desc: Trams started running in Edinburgh in 2014 and now nearly 20,000 people use them every day. Calum and his cousin Ruairidh Iain visit the city to reveal a bit more about them

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Carlota loves to race motorbikes. People say she's fast and, as a result of this, they call her Cheetah. When she coloured her hair, they renamed her the Blue Cheetah

programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC Alba

programme.title: Fonn mo Bheatha
programme.desc: Music and chat, presented by Cathy Ann MacPhee with guest Anna Murray

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Islay-born Heather Dewar travels to Mull, where she meets Eleanor MacDougall, a talented silversmith who has been running her own business on the island for many years

programme.title: Somhairle Spins
programme.desc: Somhairle Johnston introduces Niamh to bikepacking, opting for a relaxed three days cycling the Speyside Way. In Gaelic

programme.title: Rudan Beaga Bòidheach le Niamh Mackinnon
programme.desc: Niamh challenges herself to get more creative with the little things around her. In Gaelic

programme.title: Slighe Naoimh Cuithbeirt (St Cuthberts Way)
programme.desc: Iagan MacNeil finds out about the landscape and history along the St Cuthbert's Way, starting off at Melrose Abbey, where he talks to Iain Craig of Historic Environment Scotland. The 62-mile long pilgrims' route - named after the seventh-century hermit, healer and bishop St Cuthbert - links Melrose Abbey in the Scottish Borders where Cuthbert began his religious life, with his original burial place on the Holy Island of Lindisfarne off the Northumbrian coast

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: The progress of novice gardener Peter MacQueen as he transforms his back garden in Glasgow. Peter MacQueen starts preparing for the growing season, sorting through his seed collection and seeing what he has ready to plant

programme.title: Sona
programme.desc: Sauna lover Vicky Kilgour embarks on a road trip up Scotland's west coast in search of wellbeing and happiness, with friend Duncan MacDonald along for the ride. She begins by introducing her happy place, but it's the edge of his comfort zone. In Argyll, they dive into wild swimming, seaweed foraging, mud baths, salt scrubs and snorkelling, meeting incredible people along the way

programme.title: Seirm | Celtic Connections
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy presents a live music programme from Celtic Connections, featuring Blue Rose Code, Brìghde Chaimbeul & Fin Moore, and Fara

programme.title: Larach Leabhar Dheir (The Missing Monastery)
programme.desc: As the ancient Book of Deer, containing the oldest surviving example of written Scottish Gaelic, returns to its roots in the north-east of Scotland, will its presence bring success to the latest attempts by archaeologists to discover the site of the monastery in which it was written? Four years after their last attempt, a dedicated group of archaeologists and volunteers heads to an Aberdeenshire field to dig for, and painstakingly uncover, artefacts which they hope will allow the site of the Deer Monastery to finally be identified

programme.title: An Cruinne Caochlaideach (Dynamic Planet)
programme.desc: How, as the world warms, oceans are changing. This is having far-reaching consequences for the life they contain and the people that rely upon them. Journeying from the coldest waters of Antarctica to those reaching new record temperature highs in Australia, specialist teams reveal how some species are being pushed to their limits, while others are adapting, offering hope to the health of the planet's carbon-storing oceans

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Mec Lir bring the Trad Awards 2025 to a close. Featured tracks include Smash, Flashback and No Kings/No Masters

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: Fiona MacKenzie and Niall Iain Macdonald introduce the Hoosiers, who bring their infectious indie pop to the Garden Stage at Belladrum 2025. The multi-platinum band rose to fame in 2007 after the release of their debut album and singalong hits Goodbye Mr A and Worried About Ray

programme.title: An Cogadh Fuar (Cold War Stories)
programme.desc: Memories of 1983, when the world came close to nuclear war when a faulty Soviet system sent an alert of an incoming USA missile attack. Nuclear fears were again heightened in 1986 as people across the world are faced with the radioactive implications of the Chernobyl disaster. More positively, the Berlin Wall fell in 1989

programme.title: Na Sgiobairean mu Dheireadh (The Last Captains)
programme.desc: On the remote Magdalen Islands in Canada’s Gulf of St Lawrence, a centuries-old fishing way of life is under threat. In the isolated communities of Grosse Île and Old Harry, generations of fishermen have survived by working the unforgiving North Atlantic - but rising pressures, changing oceans and an uncertain future are pushing this resilient community to breaking point. This series follows determined fishermen, including Adam Clark, Steven Clark and William Burke, as they battle brutal weather, fierce competition and the relentless demands of the sea across high-stakes snow crab, scallop, lobster and tuna seasons. With livelihoods, legacies and family traditions on the line, these captains must risk everything to survive in one of the harshest and most unpredictable fishing grounds in the world

programme.title: Rathad Ramsay
programme.desc: Ramsay meets writer and producer Maureen MacLeod, just north of Glasgow to enjoy the nature right on their doorstep. The pair follow the popular West Highland Way route as Maureen talks about the importance of home and how that has shaped her career. She opens up about her passion for travel and visiting new places, but also the tough reality of travelling to hostile environments

programme.title: Trusadh - Each nan Eilean/Scotland's Native Horses

programme.title: Port

programme.title: Greenland: Sgeul Isobel Wylie Hutchison
programme.desc: Kirsty MacDonald follows in the footsteps of pioneering Scottish adventurer Isobel Wylie Hutchison. Being related to Isobel, this is a personal journey for Kirsty and she retraces part of the route Isobel took when she walked the length of the Western Isles in 1924

programme.title: Glan Fhèin
programme.desc: Old pals Katie and Angela run a cleaning company that specialises in servicing short-term lets. Business is booming and local entrepreneur and esteemed councillor Lewis Fraser has the company in his sights. Meanwhile, Betty performs pet-inspired poetry as she comes to terms with a personal loss. As Dido rushes to the hospital to give birth, Daniel does the maths on extended long-term pregnancy. Spoof documentary shadowing a company that services short-term lets. Written by and starring Hannah McKirdy and Lana Pheutan

programme.title: Dubhlain DIY (Instructions Not Included)
programme.desc: It's the final of this exciting amateur DIY competition. Derek 'Pluto' Murray, with the help of head judge Iain Spanish MacKay and guest judge Donnie Macleod, sets the final two couples one huge all-day challenge. They have done well getting this far, but the stakes are high now as they near the end of the competition. As always with this series, instructions are not included, so the couples will need to improvise and keep their heads as they try to work out how best to tackle the task in hand and create their own DIY masterpiece. They are one task away from being crowned Dùbhlain DIY champions and the cash prize of £2,000

programme.desc: As the ancient Book of Deer, containing the oldest surviving example of written Scottish Gaelic, returns to its roots in the north-east of Scotland, will its presence bring success to the latest attempts by archaeologists to discover the site of the monastery in which it was written? Four years after their last attempt, a dedicated group of archaeologists and volunteers heads to an Aberdeenshire field to dig for, and painstakingly uncover, artefacts which they hope will allow the site of the Deer Monastery to finally be identified

programme.title: An Cruinne Caochlaideach (Dynamic Planet)
programme.desc: How, as global temperatures rise, wild fires rage out of control, droughts and human-animal conflict intensify and systems destabilise. The people living and working on the frontline of change reveal how science, nature and tradition can help prepare mankind for a fast-changing future

programme.title: Belladrum 2025
programme.desc: Tide Lines make a much-anticipated return to the festival, as Fiona MacKenzie and Niall Iain Macdonald welcome the band to the Hot House Stage. Bringing their Glasgow Love Story to the Scottish Highlands, the band perform exciting new tracks and beloved hits with their fans in full voice at Belladrum 2025

programme.title: Rudan Beaga Bòidheach le Niamh Mackinnon
programme.desc: Niamh challenges herself to get more creative with the little things around her. In Gaelic

programme.title: Slighe Naoimh Cuithbeirt (St Cuthberts Way)
programme.desc: Iagan MacNeil finds out about the landscape and history along the St Cuthbert's Way, starting off at Melrose Abbey, where he talks to Iain Craig of Historic Environment Scotland. The 62-mile long pilgrims' route - named after the seventh-century hermit, healer and bishop St Cuthbert - links Melrose Abbey in the Scottish Borders where Cuthbert began his religious life, with his original burial place on the Holy Island of Lindisfarne off the Northumbrian coast

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: The progress of novice gardener Peter MacQueen as he transforms his back garden in Glasgow. Peter MacQueen starts preparing for the growing season, sorting through his seed collection and seeing what he has ready to plant

programme.title: Sona
programme.desc: Sauna lover Vicky Kilgour embarks on a road trip up Scotland's west coast in search of wellbeing and happiness, with friend Duncan MacDonald along for the ride. She begins by introducing her happy place, but it's the edge of his comfort zone. In Argyll, they dive into wild swimming, seaweed foraging, mud baths, salt scrubs and snorkelling, meeting incredible people along the way

programme.title: Seirm | Celtic Connections
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy presents a live music programme from Celtic Connections, featuring Blue Rose Code, Brìghde Chaimbeul & Fin Moore, and Fara

programme.title: Larach Leabhar Dheir (The Missing Monastery)
programme.desc: As the ancient Book of Deer, containing the oldest surviving example of written Scottish Gaelic, returns to its roots in the north-east of Scotland, will its presence bring success to the latest attempts by archaeologists to discover the site of the monastery in which it was written? Four years after their last attempt, a dedicated group of archaeologists and volunteers heads to an Aberdeenshire field to dig for, and painstakingly uncover, artefacts which they hope will allow the site of the Deer Monastery to finally be identified

programme.title: An Cruinne Caochlaideach (Dynamic Planet)
programme.desc: How, as the world warms, oceans are changing. This is having far-reaching consequences for the life they contain and the people that rely upon them. Journeying from the coldest waters of Antarctica to those reaching new record temperature highs in Australia, specialist teams reveal how some species are being pushed to their limits, while others are adapting, offering hope to the health of the planet's carbon-storing oceans

programme.title: An Cogadh Fuar (Cold War Stories)
programme.desc: Memories of 1983, when the world came close to nuclear war when a faulty Soviet system sent an alert of an incoming USA missile attack. Nuclear fears were again heightened in 1986 as people across the world are faced with the radioactive implications of the Chernobyl disaster. More positively, the Berlin Wall fell in 1989

programme.title: Na Sgiobairean mu Dheireadh (The Last Captains)
programme.desc: On the remote Magdalen Islands in Canada’s Gulf of St Lawrence, a centuries-old fishing way of life is under threat. In the isolated communities of Grosse Île and Old Harry, generations of fishermen have survived by working the unforgiving North Atlantic - but rising pressures, changing oceans and an uncertain future are pushing this resilient community to breaking point. This series follows determined fishermen, including Adam Clark, Steven Clark and William Burke, as they battle brutal weather, fierce competition and the relentless demands of the sea across high-stakes snow crab, scallop, lobster and tuna seasons. With livelihoods, legacies and family traditions on the line, these captains must risk everything to survive in one of the harshest and most unpredictable fishing grounds in the world

programme.title: Greenland: Sgeul Isobel Wylie Hutchison
programme.desc: Kirsty MacDonald follows in the footsteps of pioneering Scottish adventurer Isobel Wylie Hutchison. Being related to Isobel, this is a personal journey for Kirsty and she retraces part of the route Isobel took when she walked the length of the Western Isles in 1924

programme.title: Glan Fhèin

programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Slighe Naoimh Cuithbeirt (St Cuthberts Way)
programme.desc: Iagan MacNeil finds out about the landscape and history along the St Cuthbert's Way, starting off at Melrose Abbey, where he talks to Iain Craig of Historic Environment Scotland. The 62-mile long pilgrims' route - named after the seventh-century hermit, healer and bishop St Cuthbert - links Melrose Abbey in the Scottish Borders where Cuthbert began his religious life, with his original burial place on the Holy Island of Lindisfarne off the Northumbrian coast

programme.title: Rathad Ramsay
programme.desc: Walking along the beaches of Barra, Ramsay MacMahon talks to Michael Buchanan about his life. From his upbringing on Barra to his career as a social affairs correspondent, Michael reflects upon some of his most poignant investigations

programme.title: Larach Leabhar Dheir (The Missing Monastery)
programme.desc: As the ancient Book of Deer, containing the oldest surviving example of written Scottish Gaelic, returns to its roots in the north-east of Scotland, will its presence bring success to the latest attempts by archaeologists to discover the site of the monastery in which it was written? Four years after their last attempt, a dedicated group of archaeologists and volunteers heads to an Aberdeenshire field to dig for, and painstakingly uncover, artefacts which they hope will allow the site of the Deer Monastery to finally be identified

programme.title: An Cruinne Caochlaideach (Dynamic Planet)
programme.desc: How, as global temperatures rise, wild fires rage out of control, droughts and human-animal conflict intensify and systems destabilise. The people living and working on the frontline of change reveal how science, nature and tradition can help prepare mankind for a fast-changing future

programme.title: Belladrum 2025
programme.desc: Tide Lines make a much-anticipated return to the festival, as Fiona MacKenzie and Niall Iain Macdonald welcome the band to the Hot House Stage. Bringing their Glasgow Love Story to the Scottish Highlands, the band perform exciting new tracks and beloved hits with their fans in full voice at Belladrum 2025

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Africa's Lion Kings: Natural World
programme.desc: Revisiting the African lions that learned to prey on elephants in the drought-ridden Kalahari Desert, and catching up with the pride during the rainy season. Imogen Stubbs narrates

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough investigates the first plants to invade the land from the water 400 million years ago. He reveals how these were closely followed by animals and included the ancestors of millipedes and insects

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose tells the story of Captain James Cook's historic 1768 voyage to observe the transit of Venus, while also searching for a vast southern continent. No such land mass was discovered, but the crew of the Endeavour went on to Tahiti and Australia, which Cook claimed for Britain. The ship was almost ripped apart on the Great Barrier Reef, but the captain's navigational skills eventually steered it back home to safety

programme.title: Starter for 10
programme.desc: A working-class student struggling to fit in at a Bristol university in the 1980s applies his formidable general knowledge to get on the college's University Challenge team. An even bigger challenge presents itself as he tries to woo a team-mate. Comedy drama, starring James McAvoy, Alice Eve, Catherine Tate, Charles Dance and Lindsay Duncan, with Mark Gatiss as quizmaster Bamber Gascoigne

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard take over a country cottage for a couple of days, with unexpected results. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrew household echoes to the sound of laughter, as Margaret joins the local amateur dramatic society and Victor begins penning a sitcom script. However, his mother-in-law's bath-time accident and a run-in with British Telecom soon leave the curmudgeonly codger in no mood for jokes. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Woolly Mammoth: Secrets from the Ice
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the natural history of the ice age mammals that once roamed across what is now Europe and Asia. Detailed information about their lives has been gleaned from carcasses preserved in the Siberian permafrost, and the anatomist meets scientists using genetic, chemical and molecular tests to reveal the adaptations that allowed mammoths to evolve from tropical animals to creatures capable of surviving extreme cold

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough investigates the first plants to invade the land from the water 400 million years ago. He reveals how these were closely followed by animals and included the ancestors of millipedes and insects

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose tells the story of Captain James Cook's historic 1768 voyage to observe the transit of Venus, while also searching for a vast southern continent. No such land mass was discovered, but the crew of the Endeavour went on to Tahiti and Australia, which Cook claimed for Britain. The ship was almost ripped apart on the Great Barrier Reef, but the captain's navigational skills eventually steered it back home to safety

programme.title: David Hockney: A Life in Art - The Interview
programme.desc: In a programme from last year broadcast ahead of the opening of his biggest-ever show in Paris, the artist talks to Katie Razzall about why painting still makes him happy at 87. He also speaks about growing old, a visit from the King, and his two big loves - smoking and painting

programme.title: Face to Face with David Hockney
programme.desc: In an interview first broadcast in 1993, the artist talks to Jeremy Isaacs about painting, designing for the opera and his growing deafness

programme.title: Heligan: Secrets of the Lost Garden
programme.desc: The secret lives of the wildlife in the popular Cornish tourist attraction, set against the background of the gardening year. Charlie Hamilton James shoots footage highlighting a variety of creatures inhabiting the estate, including foxes at play in the pleasure gardens after the visitors have left, toads mating and the badgers that enter the tea rooms at night. Narrated by Philippa Forrester

programme.title: The Rite of Spring: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Ben Gernon presents from Manchester's Aviva Studios as the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra perform Stravinsky's ballet score, which famously incited audience uproar in its 1913 Paris premiere, as well as the UK premiere of Jessie Montgomery's Chemiluminescence

programme.title: BBC Proms 2017
programme.desc: Ravi Shankar and Philip Glass recorded the studio album Passages together in 1989, a unique fusion of Hindustani sitar music with American minimalism. London's Royal Albert Hall sees the album performed in full for the first time, with Shankar's daughter Anoushka playing the sitar alongside an array of Indian soloists and the Britten Sinfonia, under conductor Karen Kamensek

programme.title: Ravi Shankar - In Concert
programme.desc: The Indian musician Ravi Shankar in concert, performing just one piece, entitled Raga Bihag. First broadcast on May 15, 1974

programme.title: South Asian Music at the BBC
programme.desc: To help mark 60 years of Asian broadcasting at the BBC, Asian Network DJ Bobby Friction shares his love and knowledge of South Asian music in a look back back through the archives that celebrates some of the genre's most important and enduring acts from across the decades. With artists ranging from Lata Mangeshkar, Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan and Ravi Shankar right through to Asian Dub Foundation, Naughty Boy and Apache Indian, Bobby's selection highlights the vast range of musical styles and sounds

programme.title: Heligan: Secrets of the Lost Garden
programme.desc: The secret lives of the wildlife in the popular Cornish tourist attraction, set against the background of the gardening year. Charlie Hamilton James shoots footage highlighting a variety of creatures inhabiting the estate, including foxes at play in the pleasure gardens after the visitors have left, toads mating and the badgers that enter the tea rooms at night. Narrated by Philippa Forrester

programme.title: The Rite of Spring: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Ben Gernon presents from Manchester's Aviva Studios as the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra perform Stravinsky's ballet score, which famously incited audience uproar in its 1913 Paris premiere, as well as the UK premiere of Jessie Montgomery's Chemiluminescence

programme.title: Ravi Shankar - In Concert
programme.desc: The Indian musician Ravi Shankar in concert, performing just one piece, entitled Raga Bihag. First broadcast on May 15, 1974

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as two teams battle it out in the grand final of the student quiz, endeavouring to succeed previous winners Imperial College London and become the 52nd champions, carrying off the trophy designed by Manchester sculptor Adrian Moakes

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as Douglas Adama fans the Hitchhikers take on obscure European board games enthusiasts the Strategists in the second semi-final, competing to link elements that at first glance seem utterly random

programme.title: The Tea Trail with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the first of two This World documentaries examining the stories behind the nation's favourite beverages, adventurer and broadcaster Simon Reeve looks at the production of tea. He begins in the Kenyan coastal city of Mombasa, where most of the leaves for teabags are bought at auction, before travelling through eastern Africa to meet some of the millions of people who pick, pack and transport it. He drinks tea with everyone from Maasai cattle herders to the descendants of the original plantation owners, and hears claims of low wages and child labour

programme.title: Andy Warhol's America
programme.desc: A look at the artist's response to the Civil Rights movement and the assassination of John F. Kennedy, as well as other major historical events happening in America during the 1960s. Plus, stories about Warhol setting up the Silver Factory and managing the Velvet Underground, and also how he faced his own personal nightmare - he was shot by a Factory associate

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes continues his journey through the American art world, stopping off to examine the revolutionary styles of the 1950s and 1960s. As the USA became a global superpower, consumer culture and Cold War paranoia grew, spawning a new generation of artists. The Abstract Expressionists, including Jackson Pollock, Willem de Kooning and Mark Rothko, believed the process of creating a painting as important as the finished product, while Andy Warhol and the Pop Artists celebrated the aesthetic qualities of everyday products.

programme.title: Jacob Collier: In the Room Where It Happens - Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of the virtuoso multi-instrumentalist, singer and arranger, who has won Grammys for each of his first four albums, outdoing even the Beatles. With contributions from collaborators including Stormzy, Chris Martin and Hans Zimmer

programme.title: Stolen: Catching the Art Thieves
programme.desc: Documentary featuring first-hand accounts of Europe's most daring art heists, beginning with the theft of JMW Turner's Shade and Darkness and Light and Colour in 1994 from the Schirn gallery in Frankfurt. The paintings were on loan from the London's Tate and the invetsigation into the heist led to a high-stakes game of cat and mouse across Europe, from London to Belgrade deep into the world of Serbian warlords

programme.title: Andy Warhol's America
programme.desc: A look at the artist's response to the Civil Rights movement and the assassination of John F. Kennedy, as well as other major historical events happening in America during the 1960s. Plus, stories about Warhol setting up the Silver Factory and managing the Velvet Underground, and also how he faced his own personal nightmare - he was shot by a Factory associate

programme.title: Jacob Collier: In the Room Where It Happens - Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of the virtuoso multi-instrumentalist, singer and arranger, who has won Grammys for each of his first four albums, outdoing even the Beatles. With contributions from collaborators including Stormzy, Chris Martin and Hans Zimmer

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning at London's Marylebone railway terminus, Michael Portillo embarks on a exploration of Britain's southern counties. His first stop is Denham in Buckinghamshire, where engineers are constructing a striking new viaduct that will be the longest railway bridge in the UK, then he alights at High Wycombe, a historic centre of furniture-making thanks to its setting amid an abundance of beechwood forest

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: In the final of the quiz show, the Gamblers compete against the Strategists to try to make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Chaired by Victoria Coren

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth makes expensive vacation plans and discovers that voluntary church cleaning is certainly no holiday. Meanwhile, Rose ends up in a compromising position. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Mary Millar

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: A persistent door-to-door salesman makes grouchy Victor Meldrew see red - and an unending stream of junk mail through his letterbox threatens to push him over the brink into insanity. Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Angus Deayton star

programme.title: The Last Battle of the Vikings
programme.desc: Marine archaeologist Dr Jon Henderson tells the story of the Norsemen in Scotland, visiting archaeological sites across Scotland and Norway. He reveals that, although the battle at Largs with the Scottish army in 1263 marked the end of an era for the Norsemen, their presence continued to shape the identity and culture of the Scottish nation to the present day

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: The British Army under General Cornwallis struggles to pacify the southern states. Meanwhile, one of the most respected American generals betrays the cause and defects to the British. Supported by the French Army and Navy, Washington's Continental Army wins the victory at Yorktown. Peace is restored, independence is won, and Americans aspire for a more perfect union

programme.title: Easter Island Origins
programme.desc: A look at the most famous and mysterious monuments on the planet, located on a remote island in the middle of the Pacific. New evidence is now surfacing and radically re-writing the story of Easter Island, drawing on the latest science to answer questions such as who made the statues

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: In the second semi-final, three fans of The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy compete against a team of European board game enthusiasts to try to make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Chaired by Victoria Coren

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: In the final of the quiz show, the Gamblers compete against the Strategists to try to make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Chaired by Victoria Coren

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning at London's Marylebone railway terminus, Michael Portillo embarks on a exploration of Britain's southern counties. His first stop is Denham in Buckinghamshire, where engineers are constructing a striking new viaduct that will be the longest railway bridge in the UK, then he alights at High Wycombe, a historic centre of furniture-making thanks to its setting amid an abundance of beechwood forest

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: The British Army under General Cornwallis struggles to pacify the southern states. Meanwhile, one of the most respected American generals betrays the cause and defects to the British. Supported by the French Army and Navy, Washington's Continental Army wins the victory at Yorktown. Peace is restored, independence is won, and Americans aspire for a more perfect union

programme.title: The Tea Trail with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the first of two This World documentaries examining the stories behind the nation's favourite beverages, adventurer and broadcaster Simon Reeve looks at the production of tea. He begins in the Kenyan coastal city of Mombasa, where most of the leaves for teabags are bought at auction, before travelling through eastern Africa to meet some of the millions of people who pick, pack and transport it. He drinks tea with everyone from Maasai cattle herders to the descendants of the original plantation owners, and hears claims of low wages and child labour

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins Navy Wings pilots for a close formation flight in the skies over Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. His 1954 Harvard single-engine aircraft is flanked by a de Havilland Chipmunk and a Supermarine Seafire. He also visits a signaling manufacturer in Wiltshire that has been at the heart of British railway projects for decades

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Brian Cox asks how the universe came to be, exploring cutting-edge space missions that have revealed the origin of the universe. He also touches on the discoveries that helped scientists understand how humankind came to be here and the technology they have used along the way, from Nasa's Hubble Space Telescope to the European Space Agency's Planck space telescope

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: A revealing account of Hitler's invasion of the Soviet Union in 1941, which was a catalyst for an even more extreme persecution of the Jews. New evidence from victims, bystanders and a former member of a Nazi killing squad combine to provide a fresh insight into the decision-making processes that led to mass slaughter

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: Simon Thurley visits Hampton Court Palace to find out all about Henry VIII's famous sewerage system. First broadcast March 25, 2002

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: As punk rock grips the nation, the scattered friends return to Newcastle, where Geordie is dragged deeper into the drugs scene. Meanwhile, Nicky's lust for political power leads him to stand for Parliament, and Tosker meets the woman of his dreams. Christopher Eccleston, Daniel Craig and Mark Strong star in the generation-spanning drama

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: The miners' strike dominates the country and Mary and Tosker find themselves facing their policeman son on the picket lines. However, things pick up as Tosker makes his first million, and Nicky returns to Newcastle as a professional photographer. Christopher Eccleston, Daniel Craig, Mark Strong and Gina McKee star

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: A revealing account of Hitler's invasion of the Soviet Union in 1941, which was a catalyst for an even more extreme persecution of the Jews. New evidence from victims, bystanders and a former member of a Nazi killing squad combine to provide a fresh insight into the decision-making processes that led to mass slaughter

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. He also touches on the discoveries that helped scientists understand how humankind came to be here and the technology they have used along the way, from Nasa's Hubble Space Telescope to the European Space Agency's Planck space telescope

programme.title: TOTP: 2000
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on January 21, 1999. Featuring Pet Shop Boys, Donell Jones, Point Break, Missy 'Misdemeanor' Elliott, Eurythmics, Scooch, Justin and Manic Street Preachers.

programme.title: TOTP: 2000
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on January 28, 2000. Featuring Donell Jones, Sting feat Cheb Mami, Nu Generation, Super Furry Animals, Hi-Gate, Manic Street Preachers, Scanty Sandwich and Britney Spears

programme.title: TOTP: 1986
programme.desc: Janice Long presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on July 3, 1986. Featuring The Housemartins, Samantha Fox, Claire & Friends, Sly Fox, Gary Numan, Bucks Fizz and Wham!

programme.title: TOTP: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on July 6, featuring performances by Sonia, Monie Love, Gladys Knight, Karyn White, Chaka Khan, Pet Shop Boys and the Beautiful South

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost works on his new garden by planting shrubs and building a path, creating a potted herb garden, and suggesting wall plants for sun or shade. In Devon, Carol Klein combines flowers and edibles, and at West Dean Gardens, Jamie Butterworth shares delphinium-growing tips. Plus, inspiring rose and cottage gardens are showcased in Birmingham and Norwich

programme.title: Words and Music: American Stories 2
programme.desc: US singer-songwriters - including Jackson Browne, Nanci Griffith and Loudon Wainwright - perform one of their key songs and explain how they came to write it. First broadcast April 4, 1994

programme.title: Jackson Browne: Old Grey Whistle Test 1976
programme.desc: Bob Harris presents musician and singer-songwriter Jackson Browne in a concert from the BBC Television Theatre, London. First broadcast 7 December 1976

programme.title: Rosanne Cash, Nanci Griffith and Mary Chapin Carpenter - Bottom Line Special
programme.desc: Country singers Roseanne Cash, Nanci Griffith and Mary Chapin Carpenter in an acoustic performance at the Bottom Line Club in New York. The trio play songs including There's a Light Beyond These Woods, Rise from the Ashes, It's Too Late, and The Sun, Moon and Stars. First broadcast 29 August 1992

programme.title: Loudon Wainwright III: BBC4 Sessions
programme.desc: A performance by American singer-songwriter Loudon Wainwright III filmed at the Bush Hall during a tour of the UK. Includes tracks from his album Here Come the Choppers, as well as old favourites One Man Guy, I'm Alright and Hard Day on the Planet

programme.title: The Whistle Test Years
programme.desc: Bob Harris introduces music from 1975. Featuring performances by Little Feat, Ronnie Lane's Slim Chance, Richard and Linda Thompson, John Lennon, Billy Joel, Jackson Browne and Lynyrd Skynyrd

programme.title: TOTP: 2000
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on January 21, 1999. Featuring Pet Shop Boys, Donell Jones, Point Break, Missy 'Misdemeanor' Elliott, Eurythmics, Scooch, Justin and Manic Street Preachers.

programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of the virtuoso multi-instrumentalist, singer and arranger, who has won Grammys for each of his first four albums, outdoing even the Beatles. With contributions from collaborators including Stormzy, Chris Martin and Hans Zimmer

programme.title: Stolen: Catching the Art Thieves
programme.desc: Documentary featuring first-hand accounts of Europe's most daring art heists, beginning with the theft of JMW Turner's Shade and Darkness and Light and Colour in 1994 from the Schirn gallery in Frankfurt. The paintings were on loan from the London's Tate and the invetsigation into the heist led to a high-stakes game of cat and mouse across Europe, from London to Belgrade deep into the world of Serbian warlords

programme.title: Andy Warhol's America
programme.desc: A look at the artist's response to the Civil Rights movement and the assassination of John F. Kennedy, as well as other major historical events happening in America during the 1960s. Plus, stories about Warhol setting up the Silver Factory and managing the Velvet Underground, and also how he faced his own personal nightmare - he was shot by a Factory associate

programme.title: Jacob Collier: In the Room Where It Happens - Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of the virtuoso multi-instrumentalist, singer and arranger, who has won Grammys for each of his first four albums, outdoing even the Beatles. With contributions from collaborators including Stormzy, Chris Martin and Hans Zimmer

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning at London's Marylebone railway terminus, Michael Portillo embarks on a exploration of Britain's southern counties. His first stop is Denham in Buckinghamshire, where engineers are constructing a striking new viaduct that will be the longest railway bridge in the UK, then he alights at High Wycombe, a historic centre of furniture-making thanks to its setting amid an abundance of beechwood forest

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: In the final of the quiz show, the Gamblers compete against the Strategists to try to make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Chaired by Victoria Coren

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth makes expensive vacation plans and discovers that voluntary church cleaning is certainly no holiday. Meanwhile, Rose ends up in a compromising position. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Mary Millar

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: A persistent door-to-door salesman makes grouchy Victor Meldrew see red - and an unending stream of junk mail through his letterbox threatens to push him over the brink into insanity. Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Angus Deayton star

programme.title: The Last Battle of the Vikings
programme.desc: Marine archaeologist Dr Jon Henderson tells the story of the Norsemen in Scotland, visiting archaeological sites across Scotland and Norway. He reveals that, although the battle at Largs with the Scottish army in 1263 marked the end of an era for the Norsemen, their presence continued to shape the identity and culture of the Scottish nation to the present day

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: The British Army under General Cornwallis struggles to pacify the southern states. Meanwhile, one of the most respected American generals betrays the cause and defects to the British. Supported by the French Army and Navy, Washington's Continental Army wins the victory at Yorktown. Peace is restored, independence is won, and Americans aspire for a more perfect union

programme.title: Easter Island Origins
programme.desc: A look at the most famous and mysterious monuments on the planet, located on a remote island in the middle of the Pacific. New evidence is now surfacing and radically re-writing the story of Easter Island, drawing on the latest science to answer questions such as who made the statues

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: In the second semi-final, three fans of The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy compete against a team of European board game enthusiasts to try to make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Chaired by Victoria Coren

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: In the final of the quiz show, the Gamblers compete against the Strategists to try to make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Chaired by Victoria Coren

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning at London's Marylebone railway terminus, Michael Portillo embarks on a exploration of Britain's southern counties. His first stop is Denham in Buckinghamshire, where engineers are constructing a striking new viaduct that will be the longest railway bridge in the UK, then he alights at High Wycombe, a historic centre of furniture-making thanks to its setting amid an abundance of beechwood forest

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: The British Army under General Cornwallis struggles to pacify the southern states. Meanwhile, one of the most respected American generals betrays the cause and defects to the British. Supported by the French Army and Navy, Washington's Continental Army wins the victory at Yorktown. Peace is restored, independence is won, and Americans aspire for a more perfect union

programme.title: The Tea Trail with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the first of two This World documentaries examining the stories behind the nation's favourite beverages, adventurer and broadcaster Simon Reeve looks at the production of tea. He begins in the Kenyan coastal city of Mombasa, where most of the leaves for teabags are bought at auction, before travelling through eastern Africa to meet some of the millions of people who pick, pack and transport it. He drinks tea with everyone from Maasai cattle herders to the descendants of the original plantation owners, and hears claims of low wages and child labour

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins Navy Wings pilots for a close formation flight in the skies over Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. His 1954 Harvard single-engine aircraft is flanked by a de Havilland Chipmunk and a Supermarine Seafire. He also visits a signaling manufacturer in Wiltshire that has been at the heart of British railway projects for decades

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Brian Cox asks how the universe came to be, exploring cutting-edge space missions that have revealed the origin of the universe. He also touches on the discoveries that helped scientists understand how humankind came to be here and the technology they have used along the way, from Nasa's Hubble Space Telescope to the European Space Agency's Planck space telescope

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: A revealing account of Hitler's invasion of the Soviet Union in 1941, which was a catalyst for an even more extreme persecution of the Jews. New evidence from victims, bystanders and a former member of a Nazi killing squad combine to provide a fresh insight into the decision-making processes that led to mass slaughter

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: Simon Thurley visits Hampton Court Palace to find out all about Henry VIII's famous sewerage system. First broadcast March 25, 2002

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: As punk rock grips the nation, the scattered friends return to Newcastle, where Geordie is dragged deeper into the drugs scene. Meanwhile, Nicky's lust for political power leads him to stand for Parliament, and Tosker meets the woman of his dreams. Christopher Eccleston, Daniel Craig and Mark Strong star in the generation-spanning drama

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: The miners' strike dominates the country and Mary and Tosker find themselves facing their policeman son on the picket lines. However, things pick up as Tosker makes his first million, and Nicky returns to Newcastle as a professional photographer. Christopher Eccleston, Daniel Craig, Mark Strong and Gina McKee star

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: A revealing account of Hitler's invasion of the Soviet Union in 1941, which was a catalyst for an even more extreme persecution of the Jews. New evidence from victims, bystanders and a former member of a Nazi killing squad combine to provide a fresh insight into the decision-making processes that led to mass slaughter

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins Navy Wings pilots for a close formation flight in the skies over Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. His 1954 Harvard single-engine aircraft is flanked by a de Havilland Chipmunk and a Supermarine Seafire. He also visits a signaling manufacturer in Wiltshire that has been at the heart of British railway projects for decades

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Brian Cox asks how the universe came to be, exploring cutting-edge space missions that have revealed the origin of the universe. He also touches on the discoveries that helped scientists understand how humankind came to be here and the technology they have used along the way, from Nasa's Hubble Space Telescope to the European Space Agency's Planck space telescope

programme.title: TOTP: 2000
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on January 21, 1999. Featuring Pet Shop Boys, Donell Jones, Point Break, Missy 'Misdemeanor' Elliott, Eurythmics, Scooch, Justin and Manic Street Preachers.

programme.title: TOTP: 2000
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on January 28, 2000. Featuring Donell Jones, Sting feat Cheb Mami, Nu Generation, Super Furry Animals, Hi-Gate, Manic Street Preachers, Scanty Sandwich and Britney Spears

programme.title: TOTP: 1986
programme.desc: Janice Long presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on July 3, 1986. Featuring The Housemartins, Samantha Fox, Claire & Friends, Sly Fox, Gary Numan, Bucks Fizz and Wham!

programme.title: TOTP: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on July 6, featuring performances by Sonia, Monie Love, Gladys Knight, Karyn White, Chaka Khan, Pet Shop Boys and the Beautiful South

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost works on his new garden by planting shrubs and building a path, creating a potted herb garden, and suggesting wall plants for sun or shade. In Devon, Carol Klein combines flowers and edibles, and at West Dean Gardens, Jamie Butterworth shares delphinium-growing tips. Plus, inspiring rose and cottage gardens are showcased in Birmingham and Norwich

programme.title: Words and Music: American Stories 2
programme.desc: US singer-songwriters - including Jackson Browne, Nanci Griffith and Loudon Wainwright - perform one of their key songs and explain how they came to write it. First broadcast April 4, 1994

programme.title: Jackson Browne: Old Grey Whistle Test 1976
programme.desc: Bob Harris presents musician and singer-songwriter Jackson Browne in a concert from the BBC Television Theatre, London. First broadcast 7 December 1976

programme.title: Rosanne Cash, Nanci Griffith and Mary Chapin Carpenter - Bottom Line Special
programme.desc: Country singers Roseanne Cash, Nanci Griffith and Mary Chapin Carpenter in an acoustic performance at the Bottom Line Club in New York. The trio play songs including There's a Light Beyond These Woods, Rise from the Ashes, It's Too Late, and The Sun, Moon and Stars. First broadcast 29 August 1992

programme.title: Loudon Wainwright III: BBC4 Sessions
programme.desc: A performance by American singer-songwriter Loudon Wainwright III filmed at the Bush Hall during a tour of the UK. Includes tracks from his album Here Come the Choppers, as well as old favourites One Man Guy, I'm Alright and Hard Day on the Planet

programme.title: The Whistle Test Years
programme.desc: Bob Harris introduces music from 1975. Featuring performances by Little Feat, Ronnie Lane's Slim Chance, Richard and Linda Thompson, John Lennon, Billy Joel, Jackson Browne and Lynyrd Skynyrd

programme.title: TOTP: 2000
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on January 21, 1999. Featuring Pet Shop Boys, Donell Jones, Point Break, Missy 'Misdemeanor' Elliott, Eurythmics, Scooch, Justin and Manic Street Preachers.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: A global, topical weekly show that lifts the hood on the entertainment and news business. The Media Show explores the companies and personalities who command our attention, and how our perception of the world is shaped by their actions. From the streaming giants who entertain us, to the news outlets and social media platforms that inform and enrage us, The Media Show explains how the industry really works.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Cook and food writer Rachel Khoo visits the cool of Sweden's capital city to continue her culinary journey. Rachel visits one of Stockholm's hippest restaurants, where rump of deer is on the menu. Back home, she's inspired by Sweden's traditional cinnamon buns and bakes her own savoury Swedish buns as well as cooking venison steak with celeriac puree, pickled blackberries and carrot petals and a lingonberry and raspberry marzipan tart, inspired by the Swedish lingonberry.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Cook and food writer Rachel Khoo visits the cool of Sweden's capital city to continue her culinary journey. Rachel visits one of Stockholm's hippest restaurants, where rump of deer is on the menu. Back home, she's inspired by Sweden's traditional cinnamon buns and bakes her own savoury Swedish buns as well as cooking venison steak with celeriac puree, pickled blackberries and carrot petals and a lingonberry and raspberry marzipan tart, inspired by the Swedish lingonberry.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: A global, topical weekly show that lifts the hood on the entertainment and news business. The Media Show explores the companies and personalities who command our attention, and how our perception of the world is shaped by their actions. From the streaming giants who entertain us, to the news outlets and social media platforms that inform and enrage us, The Media Show explains how the industry really works.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Join the team on their journey of discovery as they explore new destinations around the globe and uncover hidden sides to some of the world's favourite holiday hotspots

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: House of Commons proceedings in Westminster Hall including debates on support for regenerating local high streets and removing unlawful storefronts, the contribution of nationally significant energy infrastructure projects to communities, antisemitism on university campuses, the future of high streets, and on financial inclusion for young people, from Tuesday 30 June.

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Keir Starmer, the prime minister, on Wednesday 1 July.

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the Senedd, the Welsh Parliament, to the first minister Rhun ap Iorwerth, on Tuesday 30 June.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly - Health Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of the questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly to the health minister, Mike Nesbitt, on Monday 29 June.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly - Business Rates Reform Statement
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the Northern Ireland Assembly in Stormont by finance minister John O'Dowd on the business growth accelerator and vacant premises rates reform, on Monday 29 June.

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the House of Lords to government ministers covering government contracts with the US firm Palantir, the final report of the Covid Counter Fraud Commissioner, the UK's food security, and on skills shortages in the construction sector, on Monday 29 June.

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the House of Lords to government ministers covering the ongoing pensions review, women's representation in peace processes, and on reforming university student finance, on Tuesday 30 June.

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the House of Lords to government ministers covering the role of technology in reducing drink-driving, the impact of local pharmacy closures, and on the use of AI and live facial recognition for counterterrorism purposes, on Wednesday 1 July.

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the House of Lords to government ministers covering prison family visits, per-pupil funding for 16- to 19-year-olds, and on reducing waiting times for employment tribunals, on Thursday 2 July.

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament - Nation of Sanctuary
programme.desc: Coverage of the debate in the Senedd, the Welsh Parliament, on a Reform UK motion calling on the Welsh government to scrap the 'Nation of Sanctuary' plan, on Wednesday 1 July.

programme.title: Work and Pensions/Education Committee on Child Poverty
programme.desc: Coverage of the joint session of the Work and Pensions select committee and the Education select committee on the government's child poverty strategy with evidence from a panel of parents involved with 'Changing Realities', a research and advocacy project involving over 100 parents and carers on low incomes across the UK, on Wednesday 1 July.

programme.title: Public Administration Committee on the Infected Blood Inquiry
programme.desc: Coverage of the Public Administration and Constitutional Affairs select committee session on the recommendations of the Infected Blood Inquiry with evidence from the inquiry's chair, Sir Brian Langstaff, and the counsel to the inquiry, Jenni Richards KC, on Tuesday 30 June.

programme.title: Jerusalem Holy Places Committee
programme.desc: Coverage of the Foreign Affairs committee session on potential changes to the agreements and principles that govern religious access to Holy Sites in Jerusalem, with evidence from Theophilos III, Patriarch of the Greek Orthodox Church of Jerusalem, Hosam Elias Naoum, Archbishop of the Episcopal Diocese of Jerusalem and Gilad Kariv, member of the Knesset for the Democrats, on Monday 29 June.

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament - Additional Learning Needs
programme.desc: Coverage of the debate in the Senedd, the Welsh Parliament, on funding for additional learning needs in the first Supplementary Budget 2026-27, on Wednesday 1 July.

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Keir Starmer, the prime minister, on Wednesday 1 July.

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the Senedd, the Welsh Parliament, to the first minister Rhun ap Iorwerth, on Tuesday 30 June.

programme.title: Work and Pensions Questions

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Agency: Unfiltered
programme.desc: Darby reveals a secret that has been troubling her, while Kirsten and Amy meet the three Academy finalists and ask them all their toughest questions

programme.title: The Agency: Unfiltered
programme.desc: Nicole decides whether to stay signed with the agency, Darby tells her story on Charlotte Dawson's podcast and Kirsten and Amy announce the winner of this year's Academy

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Will Kirk and Kirsten Ramsay head to Musselburgh, on Scotland's east coast. It's a town boasting a proud heritage that is celebrated each year with a procession led by their elected Honest Lad and Honest Lass. However, for the past few years, the antique carriage these local luminaries ride in has been off the road. Restoring the carriage is a huge job that requires the skills of local signwriter Ross Hastie and wheelwright Greg Rowland, and made more complicated by the impending deadline of this year's festival. A musical instrument also needs some tender loving care and Will gets a makeover at Scotland's oldest bespoke tailors

programme.title: The Chief
programme.desc: Though he is married to the force and justice is his mistress, Miekelson feels the time is right to dip his toe into romantic waters and sets out to explore the singles scene. Though hoping to meet a high-flyer at a similar level to himself, the Chief finds he has a lot to learn about modern dating etiquette. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Rhona Cameron and Lana Pheutan

programme.title: The Scotts
programme.desc: Laura is troubled by the fact that one of the regular customers at the cafe where she works is the spitting image of a Hollywood star. The extended family chips in with advice as Henry attempts to keep a lid on his jealousy. Meanwhile, Darren joins Collette on her mission to inspire pregnant women. Comedy, starring Robert Florence and Iain Connell

programme.title: My Old School
programme.desc: The story of 17-year-old Brandon Lee, who in 1993 enrolled at Bearsden Academy secondary school in a well-to-do suburb of Glasgow. He turned out to be a model pupil, surprising his teachers with a wealth of knowledge beyond his years. He took fellow students under his wing and even starred in the school production of South Pacific. But then his unbelievable secret was revealed. This documentary tells the incredible story of a man who lived his school days twice, with Alan Cumming and Lulu helping to bring the tale to life

programme.title: Growing Up Scottish
programme.desc: Comedians and entertainers look back at friendships, reminiscing about the bonding, the hanging out, the best friends, the sleepovers and the strange noises

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Prestwick's art deco Broadway Cinema has not shown a film since its doors closed in 1976, so Mark Stuckey and Steve Rowley must battle rust, missing wires and obsolete carbon-arc lamps if they hope to bring its twin projectors back to life. Dom Chinea meets Shetlander Victoria, who needs her grandfather's accordion fixed if she's to realise her busking ambitions, and then discovers the centuries-old art of enamelling watch dials, a craft that demands steady hands

programme.title: Island Crossings
programme.desc: Twenty new apprentices join the ranks of ferry operators Caledonian MacBrayne and take a tour of the MV Caledonian Isles, where they get a glimpse into living and working at sea. It's the final cattle sale of the year on the Inner Hebridean island of Tiree and local crofters are getting their cattle ready for market

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Stevie and Pete of the Special Operations Response Team join the other emergency services to help a man suffering a mental-health crisis who is threatening to harm himself in a public place. Dunoon-based paramedic Ben responds to a potentially life-threatening emergency in the shape of a man who has blood a clot in his lungs

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Jealous of Old Tam's competition-winning success, Jack and Victor try to get in on the act and create a memorable catchphrase for scones. Sitcom, starring Ford Kiernan and Greg Hemphill

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Chief Miekelson has a boozy reunion with retired police chief Sir Neville Archibald, but he is left to foot the extortionate bill. Volunteer officer Ken Beattie takes part in the local 'Night Owl' crime initiative, and officers Charlie McIntosh and Jane Mackay find themselves in hot pursuit in the great outdoors

programme.title: The Troops
programme.desc: The Royal Regiment of Scotland faces off in Estonia against Nato allies posing as enemy forces, with Maka and his fellow soldiers holding their ground under sustained fire. Back in Catterick, a different kind of battle unfolds as Laura and Josh undergo Public Order Training, a high-pressure exercise designed to prepare them for the unpredictable realities of deployment

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Nick and his father Derek get some bad news, putting plans for taking over the family farm into sharp focus in Dumfries and Galloway. They're losing 300 acres of leased grazing land to forestry. With concerns over when dad might retire, Nick sets out a timeline for his succession. On Papa Westray, new farmers Paul and Mel are in the midst of their first big lambing season. However, Paul has to turn to the wider community for help with a ewe in distress. Meanwhile at Hillhead farm near Glasgow, Ben and Matt race to get the wedding and events site ready on time

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Lizzie Schofield and Brian Cunningham start new projects and check on the progress of planting carried out in previous years. Lizzie is in the herb garden that she renovated in 2024, assessing what has worked and what needs refreshed, while Brian checks on the apple trees he planted in the spring as examples of espalier shaping and pruning. Plus, a look at what plants are good for attracting birds to gardens

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on Scottish beef, cooking an Aberdeen Angus fillet steak hache with a green peppercorn sauce and creamed spinach. Joining them are Fife buffalo farmer Steve Mitchell, and food and drink writer Gabriella Bennett, who's chosen a spicy beer from the Borders to accompany the beef

programme.title: The River
programme.desc: Farrier David Merry has the task of seeing to all the horses at the Jedburgh common ride, where Katie Scott faces a former Grand National winner in a chase to the top of the hill. Biologist James Hunt is electro-fishing to find evidence that the salmon and trout spawning grounds have survived the winter floods, while Ruth Hinks finally launches her new chocolate school with a royal twist and farmer Robert Wilson selects the best of his herd of cattle for the Kelso show

programme.title: The River
programme.desc: The Floors Stud team prepares for the Tattersalls auctions at Newmarket, the most important day of the year, but the horses need conditioning so some lunge training is required. In Peebles, Fiona and Neil Dalgleish are organising a mountain-bike race for professional riders in Glentress Forest, while ghillie Colin Bell strims more grassy river banks and sees to his summer fishing clients

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and interiors guru Michelle Ogundehin welcome a new group of wannabe designers to the Brighton Design Studio, where each week they are challenged to transform a selection of vastly different spaces. They begin on the coast in Dorset, where they must transform basic wooden beach huts into stylish spaces which also show off their signature styles - while also taking into account sustainability. To judge their efforts, Michelle is joined by leading designer Jonathan Adler

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: A determined DCI Douglas is interrogating Harry when he is stunned to receive new evidence, while a bitter Caitlin takes action when Scarlett and Sam return from Nashville. Jodie teaches Callum and Shak a lesson, and Gillian is concerned over Mikey's response to the press

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: Alan's brother is getting married, and he is the best man, although it turns out nobody has any confidence in his abilities to do anything other than order two puddings at the meal. With the rings to keep safe, a list of chores to do and Colin showing off his sexy stripper moves, a pre-ceremony gathering soon descends into chaos

programme.title: The Chief
programme.desc: Though he is married to the force and justice is his mistress, Miekelson feels the time is right to dip his toe into romantic waters and sets out to explore the singles scene. Though hoping to meet a high-flyer at a similar level to himself, the Chief finds he has a lot to learn about modern dating etiquette. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Rhona Cameron and Lana Pheutan

programme.title: Janey and Ashley Get a Real Job
programme.desc: Janey Godley and Ashley Storrie try their hands at various jobs in a factory that produces everything from hand lotion to shower gel and soap to candles. From restocking the on-site shop to making soap and filling bottles, everything must be uniform and be produced at speed with very little room for error

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major head to Angus and the town of Arbroath, a fine red sandstone town which nestles around its historic abbey. No longer with a fishing industry, the town relies on leisure boats visiting the beautiful little harbour, and Ian and Major have the chance to make pizza, bottle some rum and ride the dodgems

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Fife, Andy and Jo prepare to compete at one of the biggest highlights in the farming calendar, the Royal Highland Show. In Caithness, it's shearing time for Sally and champion shearer Helga. Friends of old, the two women have competed together in New Zealand. Things are a lot trickier now with children in the mix as they wrestle sheep while trying to keep the kids amused. At Hillhead Farm, the first event of the season is imminent prmpting the entire Imrie family to pitch in to get the marquee up and the event site finished

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: How a 16-year-old used his job in a call centre in Glasgow to go on a five-year crime wave, defrauding innocent cardholders and laying the groundwork for a life of wealth, first-class travel and an escape from the life he was destined for

programme.title: Growing Up Scottish
programme.desc: Comedians and entertainers reflect on Scottish art and the appreciation of it, from finger painting to the Turner Prize via Mackintosh and the Kelpies. Plus, the highs and lows of new town sculpture, how to appreciate a gallery, and what might be hidden away beyond the gift shop. Featuring contributions from Karen Bartke, Reuben Joseph, Brian Taylor, Des Clarke, TJ Singh, Stuart Leon, Jane McCarry, Leo Kearse, Raymond Mearns and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: The Troops
programme.desc: As Operation Hedgehog reaches its dramatic conclusion in Estonia, the Scots Guards brace for a final assault, with Maka and the Jocks digging deep - both physically and mentally - to hold their position. As they prepare for deployment to Cyprus, Laura faces the difficult task of saying goodbye to her family, while Josh shares a final celebration with loved ones before his imminent departure

programme.title: Jules and Greg's Wild Swim
programme.desc: Paddle-boarding to a hidden lagoon on Loch Trool, Julie Wilson Nimmo and Greg Hemphill spend the night in a yurt beneath Galloway's dark skies, then break for the coast and a very important stop for 'chips 'n' dips' in Girvan

programme.title: The Scotts
programme.desc: Laura is troubled by the fact that one of the regular customers at the cafe where she works is the spitting image of a Hollywood star. The extended family chips in with advice as Henry attempts to keep a lid on his jealousy. Meanwhile, Darren joins Collette on her mission to inspire pregnant women. Comedy, starring Robert Florence and Iain Connell

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year 2024
programme.desc: Architect Danny Campbell and interior designers Anna Campbell-Jones and Banjo Beale take a look at the best houses in the country, beginning by focusing on the North East and Northern Isles. Properties in the running are a modern self-build near Inverurie, a cottage in Banchory that dates back to the 1860s and a listed farmhouse in southern Aberdeenshire

programme.title: Island Crossings
programme.desc: Twenty new apprentices join the ranks of ferry operators Caledonian MacBrayne and take a tour of the MV Caledonian Isles, where they get a glimpse into living and working at sea. It's the final cattle sale of the year on the Inner Hebridean island of Tiree and local crofters are getting their cattle ready for market

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: Between the ages of 17 and 21, Elliot Castro managed to commit fraudulent activity amounting to a estimated £2 million. This documentary continues his story. With a trail of crime stretching across several continents as he continued to live life on the run, then 18-year-old Elliot became more paranoid than ever and a stint in foreign jail seemed just around the corner

programme.title: Growing Up Scottish
programme.desc: Scotland's comedians look back at sport and sporting heroes, from cross-country to tug-o-war, via the mesmerising athletics of darts and the eerie silence of golf. Plus, the art of appreciating football and deciding on your favourite Murray.With contributions by Jim Smith, Wendy Wason, Bash the Entertainer, Jojo Sutherland, Louise McCarthy & Gayle Telfer Stevens, Grado, Julia Sutherland, Amy Irons and Chris Forbes.

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Alex hits the bottle when he is banned from Karen's funeral, resulting in a confrontation and a shocking discovery. Meanwhile, Alice reaches breaking point with Mackenzie's behaviour, Maggie and Eddie try to help their loved ones, and Gillian pays Mulvaney a visit

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Brendan O'Carroll returns as Agnes Brown, the loudest, proudest woman in Ireland. Simon Delaney guest stars, with Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: David Wilson's Crime Files
programme.desc: The criminologist discusses infamous cases with experts, beginning with an analysis of Scottish serial killers Peter Manuel and Peter Tobin, examining whether or not Manuel was criminally insane and if Tobin could be Bible John who was never caugh. He talks with crime fiction author Alex Gray and conducts an in-depth interview with criminal defence lawyer Donald Findlay, QC

programme.title: Scotland's Greatest Escape

First up, it's the Hideaway Experience deep in the Angus countryside on Scotland's east coast, Dunoon and Argyll Cruising, and Glenapp Castle on the Ayrshire coast
