programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Agency: Unfiltered
programme.desc: Darby reveals a secret that has been troubling her, while Kirsten and Amy meet the three Academy finalists and ask them all their toughest questions

programme.title: The Agency: Unfiltered
programme.desc: Nicole decides whether to stay signed with the agency, Darby tells her story on Charlotte Dawson's podcast and Kirsten and Amy announce the winner of this year's Academy

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Will Kirk and Kirsten Ramsay head to Musselburgh, on Scotland's east coast. It's a town boasting a proud heritage that is celebrated each year with a procession led by their elected Honest Lad and Honest Lass. However, for the past few years, the antique carriage these local luminaries ride in has been off the road. Restoring the carriage is a huge job that requires the skills of local signwriter Ross Hastie and wheelwright Greg Rowland, and made more complicated by the impending deadline of this year's festival. A musical instrument also needs some tender loving care and Will gets a makeover at Scotland's oldest bespoke tailors

programme.title: The Chief
programme.desc: Though he is married to the force and justice is his mistress, Miekelson feels the time is right to dip his toe into romantic waters and sets out to explore the singles scene. Though hoping to meet a high-flyer at a similar level to himself, the Chief finds he has a lot to learn about modern dating etiquette. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Rhona Cameron and Lana Pheutan

programme.title: The Scotts
programme.desc: Laura is troubled by the fact that one of the regular customers at the cafe where she works is the spitting image of a Hollywood star. The extended family chips in with advice as Henry attempts to keep a lid on his jealousy. Meanwhile, Darren joins Collette on her mission to inspire pregnant women. Comedy, starring Robert Florence and Iain Connell

programme.title: My Old School
programme.desc: The story of 17-year-old Brandon Lee, who in 1993 enrolled at Bearsden Academy secondary school in a well-to-do suburb of Glasgow. He turned out to be a model pupil, surprising his teachers with a wealth of knowledge beyond his years. He took fellow students under his wing and even starred in the school production of South Pacific. But then his unbelievable secret was revealed. This documentary tells the incredible story of a man who lived his school days twice, with Alan Cumming and Lulu helping to bring the tale to life

programme.title: Growing Up Scottish
programme.desc: Comedians and entertainers look back at friendships, reminiscing about the bonding, the hanging out, the best friends, the sleepovers and the strange noises

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Prestwick's art deco Broadway Cinema has not shown a film since its doors closed in 1976, so Mark Stuckey and Steve Rowley must battle rust, missing wires and obsolete carbon-arc lamps if they hope to bring its twin projectors back to life. Dom Chinea meets Shetlander Victoria, who needs her grandfather's accordion fixed if she's to realise her busking ambitions, and then discovers the centuries-old art of enamelling watch dials, a craft that demands steady hands

programme.title: Island Crossings
programme.desc: Twenty new apprentices join the ranks of ferry operators Caledonian MacBrayne and take a tour of the MV Caledonian Isles, where they get a glimpse into living and working at sea. It's the final cattle sale of the year on the Inner Hebridean island of Tiree and local crofters are getting their cattle ready for market

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Stevie and Pete of the Special Operations Response Team join the other emergency services to help a man suffering a mental-health crisis who is threatening to harm himself in a public place. Dunoon-based paramedic Ben responds to a potentially life-threatening emergency in the shape of a man who has blood a clot in his lungs

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Jealous of Old Tam's competition-winning success, Jack and Victor try to get in on the act and create a memorable catchphrase for scones. Sitcom, starring Ford Kiernan and Greg Hemphill

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Chief Miekelson has a boozy reunion with retired police chief Sir Neville Archibald, but he is left to foot the extortionate bill. Volunteer officer Ken Beattie takes part in the local 'Night Owl' crime initiative, and officers Charlie McIntosh and Jane Mackay find themselves in hot pursuit in the great outdoors

programme.title: The Troops
programme.desc: The Royal Regiment of Scotland faces off in Estonia against Nato allies posing as enemy forces, with Maka and his fellow soldiers holding their ground under sustained fire. Back in Catterick, a different kind of battle unfolds as Laura and Josh undergo Public Order Training, a high-pressure exercise designed to prepare them for the unpredictable realities of deployment

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Nick and his father Derek get some bad news, putting plans for taking over the family farm into sharp focus in Dumfries and Galloway. They're losing 300 acres of leased grazing land to forestry. With concerns over when dad might retire, Nick sets out a timeline for his succession. On Papa Westray, new farmers Paul and Mel are in the midst of their first big lambing season. However, Paul has to turn to the wider community for help with a ewe in distress. Meanwhile at Hillhead farm near Glasgow, Ben and Matt race to get the wedding and events site ready on time

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Lizzie Schofield and Brian Cunningham start new projects and check on the progress of planting carried out in previous years. Lizzie is in the herb garden that she renovated in 2024, assessing what has worked and what needs refreshed, while Brian checks on the apple trees he planted in the spring as examples of espalier shaping and pruning. Plus, a look at what plants are good for attracting birds to gardens

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on Scottish beef, cooking an Aberdeen Angus fillet steak hache with a green peppercorn sauce and creamed spinach. Joining them are Fife buffalo farmer Steve Mitchell, and food and drink writer Gabriella Bennett, who's chosen a spicy beer from the Borders to accompany the beef

programme.title: The River
programme.desc: Farrier David Merry has the task of seeing to all the horses at the Jedburgh common ride, where Katie Scott faces a former Grand National winner in a chase to the top of the hill. Biologist James Hunt is electro-fishing to find evidence that the salmon and trout spawning grounds have survived the winter floods, while Ruth Hinks finally launches her new chocolate school with a royal twist and farmer Robert Wilson selects the best of his herd of cattle for the Kelso show

programme.title: The River
programme.desc: The Floors Stud team prepares for the Tattersalls auctions at Newmarket, the most important day of the year, but the horses need conditioning so some lunge training is required. In Peebles, Fiona and Neil Dalgleish are organising a mountain-bike race for professional riders in Glentress Forest, while ghillie Colin Bell strims more grassy river banks and sees to his summer fishing clients

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and interiors guru Michelle Ogundehin welcome a new group of wannabe designers to the Brighton Design Studio, where each week they are challenged to transform a selection of vastly different spaces. They begin on the coast in Dorset, where they must transform basic wooden beach huts into stylish spaces which also show off their signature styles - while also taking into account sustainability. To judge their efforts, Michelle is joined by leading designer Jonathan Adler

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: A determined DCI Douglas is interrogating Harry when he is stunned to receive new evidence, while a bitter Caitlin takes action when Scarlett and Sam return from Nashville. Jodie teaches Callum and Shak a lesson, and Gillian is concerned over Mikey's response to the press

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: Alan's brother is getting married, and he is the best man, although it turns out nobody has any confidence in his abilities to do anything other than order two puddings at the meal. With the rings to keep safe, a list of chores to do and Colin showing off his sexy stripper moves, a pre-ceremony gathering soon descends into chaos

programme.title: The Chief
programme.desc: Though he is married to the force and justice is his mistress, Miekelson feels the time is right to dip his toe into romantic waters and sets out to explore the singles scene. Though hoping to meet a high-flyer at a similar level to himself, the Chief finds he has a lot to learn about modern dating etiquette. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Rhona Cameron and Lana Pheutan

programme.title: Janey and Ashley Get a Real Job
programme.desc: Janey Godley and Ashley Storrie try their hands at various jobs in a factory that produces everything from hand lotion to shower gel and soap to candles. From restocking the on-site shop to making soap and filling bottles, everything must be uniform and be produced at speed with very little room for error

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major head to Angus and the town of Arbroath, a fine red sandstone town which nestles around its historic abbey. No longer with a fishing industry, the town relies on leisure boats visiting the beautiful little harbour, and Ian and Major have the chance to make pizza, bottle some rum and ride the dodgems

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Fife, Andy and Jo prepare to compete at one of the biggest highlights in the farming calendar, the Royal Highland Show. In Caithness, it's shearing time for Sally and champion shearer Helga. Friends of old, the two women have competed together in New Zealand. Things are a lot trickier now with children in the mix as they wrestle sheep while trying to keep the kids amused. At Hillhead Farm, the first event of the season is imminent prmpting the entire Imrie family to pitch in to get the marquee up and the event site finished

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: How a 16-year-old used his job in a call centre in Glasgow to go on a five-year crime wave, defrauding innocent cardholders and laying the groundwork for a life of wealth, first-class travel and an escape from the life he was destined for

programme.title: Growing Up Scottish
programme.desc: Comedians and entertainers reflect on Scottish art and the appreciation of it, from finger painting to the Turner Prize via Mackintosh and the Kelpies. Plus, the highs and lows of new town sculpture, how to appreciate a gallery, and what might be hidden away beyond the gift shop. Featuring contributions from Karen Bartke, Reuben Joseph, Brian Taylor, Des Clarke, TJ Singh, Stuart Leon, Jane McCarry, Leo Kearse, Raymond Mearns and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: The Troops
programme.desc: As Operation Hedgehog reaches its dramatic conclusion in Estonia, the Scots Guards brace for a final assault, with Maka and the Jocks digging deep - both physically and mentally - to hold their position. As they prepare for deployment to Cyprus, Laura faces the difficult task of saying goodbye to her family, while Josh shares a final celebration with loved ones before his imminent departure

programme.title: Jules and Greg's Wild Swim
programme.desc: Paddle-boarding to a hidden lagoon on Loch Trool, Julie Wilson Nimmo and Greg Hemphill spend the night in a yurt beneath Galloway's dark skies, then break for the coast and a very important stop for 'chips 'n' dips' in Girvan

programme.title: The Scotts

Comedy, starring Robert Florence and Iain Connell

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year 2024
programme.desc: Architect Danny Campbell and interior designers Anna Campbell-Jones and Banjo Beale take a look at the best houses in the country, beginning by focusing on the North East and Northern Isles. Properties in the running are a modern self-build near Inverurie, a cottage in Banchory that dates back to the 1860s and a listed farmhouse in southern Aberdeenshire

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Doctor and Clara arrive at an underwater base located at the bottom of a lake. The crew have recovered a derelict spaceship from the depths, but since bringing it on board, the station has been overrun with what appear to be ghosts. As the spectral creatures prey on the crew to boost their numbers, the Doctor must determine what they really are and what they want

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The underwater base is overrun by the ghosts, while the Doctor travels back into the past to learn the origins of the derelict spaceship. Arriving in a military outpost that will soon become the lake where the station is when a dam bursts, he encounters an alien warlord making desperate plans to ensure his survival. Guest starring Morven Christie and Paul Kaye

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: It's been over a year since Frank's death and Wendy, rediscovering a long lost love for painting, decides to create a very unique tribute to him in oils. The usually curmudgeonly Geoffrey seems very keen on the commemorative artwork, but Lee and Lucy simply hate it. Once it's hanging on their wall, the pair contrive to 'lose' the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Deborah Grant and Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the silly mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she read it. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira and David try to earn some money by selling cosmetics to the locals, while Johnny heads to Elmdale in the hope of collecting unemployment insurance

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny reluctantly agrees to speak at the funeral of a local man he did not really know, while Alexis struggles with her new boyfriend's love of animals

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira go to Roland's annual Hawaiian party, Alexis and Ted host a couples' dinner party, and David and Stevie's relationship heats up

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him and he locks himself away in a cupboard, so it is up to Agnes, Father Quinn and best man Buster to try to calm him down. Meanwhile, Rory's secret is revealed to his mother. Bawdy comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Paddy Houlihan

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Worried about his dad's health, Richard battles officious receptionists - and a stubborn Ken - to get him a same-day appointment with Dr Roach. Later, Richard gets all-star struck when he learns his acting idol Lydia Bartlett is in Forres filming, while Emily suggests he imparts his wisdom to the local am-dram group. Guest starring Samantha Spiro

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard embarks on a journey to gather together some of Ken's nearest and dearest for his surprise 75th birthday party. However, tracking down the elusive Brian - Ken's oldest friend whom he lost touch with years ago - proves quite the challenge. Elsewhere, Richard's self-taped audition for a major role is troublesome and he's lost for words when Emily admits she's going on a date with a guy called Dave. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: While Richard goes to great lengths to hide the fact he's Norway-bound for his big TV break, Ken busies himself planning the special spot to scatter his beloved Jean's ashes - even if that means a less-than-sporting response from Rod at the football ground. As well as being a decoy for Richard's departure, Emily is on hand to offer advice when things go awry for the Pritchards. Comedy, starring Greg McHugh, Gregor Fisher and Amy Lennnox

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Comedy drama about a British bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, focusing on the camaraderie, bonds and banter the soldiers in unit Bluestone 42 share, despite the dangers they face in their work. The team faces a difficult roadside operation, while new padre Mary grabs the attention of expert Nick. Oliver Chris, Stephen Wight and Kelly Adams star

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick is desperate to find decent food to use in an attempt to seduce Mary. Meanwhile, Simon gets into trouble on roadside operations. Comedy drama about a British bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, starring Oliver Chris, Stephen Wright and Kelly Adams

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: The team joins a midnight raid on a bomb factory, but discover more than they expected. Meanwhile, Simon tries to get a book deal for his memoir, and an aid worker is kidnapped. Comedy drama about a bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, starring Stephen Wright and Oliver Chris

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Mary reveals a secret about her life before she was a padre - and Nick intends to take full advantage of what she discloses. Meanwhile, Bird is offered a promotion. Comedy about a British bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, starring Kelly Adams, Oliver Chris and Katie Lyons

programme.title: World Cup 1998
programme.desc: Argentina v England. Brief highlights of the round-of-16 match from Stade Geoffroy-Guichard in Saint-Étienne, France, including a stunning solo goal and an infamous red card

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Fox-Truemans rally together, Priya questions Max's intentions, and Will tries to help Billy in his battle with Bea

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sheila attempts to win Chelsea over, Priya clashes with Cindy, and Avani grows suspicious of her mum

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max is left rattled by Priya's behaviour, Cindy and Max toast their engagement in the Vic, and Bea's influence intensifies

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea's behaviour leaves Honey concerned, Cindy clashes with Zoe over business rivalry, and Walford celebrates Pride

programme.title: Rooney
programme.desc: Documentary providing access to the life and career of former England striker and current Birmingham City manager Wayne Rooney. The programme examines his playing days with Manchester United and the national team and delves into his off-field life as he discusses his most important moments. With guest appearances from other famous footballers, including Thierry Henry, David Beckham and Rio Ferdinand

programme.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
programme.desc: A look at the careers of Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, widely regarded as the finest players of their generation, and long-time rivals at Barcelona and Real Madrid respectively. The two players have won the Ballon d'or 13 times between them, with Messi winning eight of them, and they both head to this World Cup for what is likely to be the final time

programme.title: World Cup 2022
programme.desc: Argentina v France. Action from the 2022 World Cup final at Lusail Stadium in Qatar

programme.title: Desmond Armstrong: America's World Cup Pioneer
programme.desc: The story of the first black player to represent the United States men's national football team at a World Cup and part of the squad that qualified for Italia 90

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the world's biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs' inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
programme.desc: A look at the careers of Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, widely regarded as the finest players of their generation, and long-time rivals at Barcelona and Real Madrid respectively. programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In Australia's Northern Territories, Steve Backshall encounters one of nature's most deadly predators - the saltwater crocodile

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond tackle the traffic chaos snowy weather causes in Britain, and hit upon a solution involving a combine harvester, some rudimentary engineering skills and a trip to Norway. Jeremy also tests the Audi RS5 and the BMW M3, and American actress and model Amber Heard is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The story of Kilmarnock nurse Adele Rennie, who pretended to be handsome, professional men to target, harass, stalk and exploit hundreds of women. When Dr David Graham starts flirting with former beauty queen Abbie Draper online, she turns sleuth and uncovers a trail of deception, crazy coincidences and stories that just don't add up

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Abbie Draper takes her findings to the police, but they are unable to do anything about it and the Catfish of Kilmarnock remains free to target more and more victims. However, Abbie cannot walk away and steps up her investigation over months that turn into years, with victims spread across the country and dating back over a decade

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The victims finally have justice, but are still left urgently searching for answers. What drives someone, with a seemingly normal life to terrorise countless women from the same town? Those closest to the perpetrator, who are also trying to understand the mystery behind the repeated cycles of offending, share their thoughts

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: The case of an online catfishing abuse incident, which saw a 20-year-old suspect accused by a 12-year-old girl in Scotland. The suspect's bail conditions were supposed to prevent him from accessing the internet, but he very quickly was the subject of a second accusation from New Zealand, with online activity traced back to his house

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Police re-arrest Alexander McCartney after discovering thousands of images of child abuse on his electronic devices and a multi-agency international investigation begins when the US Department of Homeland Security learn of two more catfishing victims. With McCartney due in court to plead guilty to charges of making indecent images of children and blackmail, the case takes a turn when an obituary page for one of his victims is found

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Police connect the suicide of a 12-year-old girl in West Virginia to Alexander McCartney, opening a manslaughter investigation but in order to charge him they have to prove he foresaw the risk to her life. The case sets a new legal precedent in the UK and more than five years after the investigation began, McCartney is finally convicted and sentenced

programme.title: Liar: The Fake Grooming Scandal
programme.desc: An investigation into the false allegations of rape, grooming and trafficking made by Ellie Williams in 2020 and the backlash faced by police and Asian people in Barrow-in-Furness after her arrest. When the 22-year-old's post detailing the sexual and physical assaults she claimed to have suffered at the hands of an Asian grooming gang went viral, thousands demanded action, but were shocked to hear Ellie herself had been arrested - prompting attacks on local Asian-owned businesses and demonstrations in other northern cities

programme.title: Disclosure: Should I Delete Instagram?
programme.desc: Ellis Horne investigates the effects Instagram has on mental health, meeting young people who feel their mental health has been negatively impacted by constant exposure to the perfect lives of others. She explores the role that influencers play in creating enviable lifestyles that are unobtainable to most, and asks experts how to have a healthier relationship with the social media platform

programme.title: World Cup 2022
programme.desc: Netherlands v Argentina. Brief highlights of the quarter-final from Lusail Stadium in Qatar

programme.title: Rooney
programme.desc: Documentary providing access to the life and career of former England striker and current Birmingham City manager Wayne Rooney. The programme examines his playing days with Manchester United and the national team and delves into his off-field life as he discusses his most important moments. With guest appearances from other famous footballers, including Thierry Henry, David Beckham and Rio Ferdinand

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads for India, where he goes on the trail of the saw-scaled viper. He also gets too close to the razor-sharp claws of the sloth bear

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts help more families declutter their homes, beginning with Tom and Chloe Watling and their two daughters. All their possessions are packed up and laid out in a giant warehouse, including 459 books and magazines, 326 pieces of baking equipment, 39 unopened travel-sized jars of jam and 1,672 toys. Back at the house, carpenter Rob sorts out Tom's new office and the girls' playroom, cleaner Iwan gives the home a supersize spring clean, and Dilly meticulously organises their extended kitchen and resurrects the neglected bakery

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: One diva makes a dramatic exit, prompting others to rethink how to spend their remaining time. With new connections still in play, one relationship hits the end of the road

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: A game-changing Kiss-Off ends in tears, and a Chemistry Test offers focus to those remaining, and for one lucky couple, Cupid grants the ultimate wish. A quest involving crystals doesn't quite hold the sparkle one diva was hoping for

programme.title: I Am Legend
programme.desc: A Manhattan-based military scientist finds himself immune to a virus that turns humans into ferocious vampire-like creatures. After the disease has swept across the world, wiping out an estimated 90 per cent of the human population, he devotes his solitary existence to finding a cure by unlocking the secret within his blood. Sci-fi thriller, starring Will Smith, Alice Braga and Charlie Tahan

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond tackle the traffic chaos snowy weather causes in Britain, and hit upon a solution involving a combine harvester, some rudimentary engineering skills and a trip to Norway. Jeremy also tests the Audi RS5 and the BMW M3, and American actress and model Amber Heard is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: World Cup 2014
programme.desc: Spain v Netherlands. Brief highlights of the Group B match at Itaipava Arena Fonte Nova in Salvador, Brazil

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: Now that Jamma's in a committed relationship, everyone starts to fancy him. However, instead of focusing on his own issues, he ends up playing therapist to his parents. At work he attempts to bond with Isaac to land the next big project. Sitcom, written by and starring Mawaan Rizwan

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: Guy is eager to meet Jamma's parents, but Jamma faces a challenge in getting his mother to agree to the introduction. He gets theatrical to try to uncover the truth behind Farida's actions - but is he prepared for what he'll discover? Russell Tovey stars

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: To outshine his brother, Jamma takes on too many work commitments. He also secures a flat with Winnie, but dreads the inevitable conversation with Guy. And with Saif mysteriously absent, Jamma embarks on a quest to find him, but as he searches for answers, he finds himself preoccupied by what lurks beneath the floorboards at the Megacentre

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: Jamma and his colleagues embark on an escape room adventure to foster team bonding after a disastrous pitch. Jamma tries to keep Winnie and Guy from talking to each other in hopes of covering up his lie, but instead finds himself entering a gateway into the past. Comedy, starring Mawaan Rizwan

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts help more families declutter their homes, beginning with Tom and Chloe Watling and their two daughters. All their possessions are packed up and laid out in a giant warehouse, including 459 books and magazines, 326 pieces of baking equipment, 39 unopened travel-sized jars of jam and 1,672 toys. Back at the house, carpenter Rob sorts out Tom's new office and the girls' playroom, cleaner Iwan gives the home a supersize spring clean, and Dilly meticulously organises their extended kitchen and resurrects the neglected bakery

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In the waters off the coast of Borneo, Steve Backshall comes face to face with a venomous scorpion fish and witnesses the spear-mantis impaling its supper

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of a Jaguar XJ and races against the rotation of the Earth. James May travels to America to drive a Nasa Space Exploration Vehicle and Richard Hammond tests two of the most famous supercars of the 1980s - the Porsche 959 and the Ferrari F40. John Prescott is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: England 2006: The Golden Generation
programme.desc: Documentary exploring why the men's England football team did not fulfil their potential at the 2006 FIFA World Cup. Sven-Goran Eriksson's talented side was captained by the talismanic David Beckham, but the players and their partners came under increasing scrutiny off the pitch, and they were eventually eliminated by Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal. With contributions from their key members of the squad, including Rio Ferdinand, Wayne Rooney, Ashley Cole, Owen Hargreaves and Paul Robinson

programme.title: Rooney
programme.desc: Documentary examining the life and career of Wayne Rooney, from his professional debut at the age of 16 with Everton to his goal-scoring exploits with Manchester United and England

programme.title: David Brent: Life on the Road
programme.desc: More than a decade on from his appearance in documentary series The Office, a film crew is once again following the life of David Brent. He is now working as a rep for a cleaning-goods distributor, but harbours dreams of making it big in the music business. Acting on this, he takes three weeks off work and tours with a new-look version of Foregone Conclusion, the band he fronted in the 1990s. Mockumentary comedy, starring Ricky Gervais, Ben Bailey Smith and Tom Basden

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: World Cup 2018
programme.desc: France v Croatia. Action from the final at Luzhniki Stadium in Moscow

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of a Jaguar XJ and races against the rotation of the Earth. James May travels to America to drive a Nasa Space Exploration Vehicle and Richard Hammond tests two of the most famous supercars of the 1980s - the Porsche 959 and the Ferrari F40. John Prescott is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Dance Race
programme.desc: New Zealand choreographer Corey Baker's short film which a range of dancers, from ballet and Strictly stars to choreographed skydivers and drivers, race across the UK to meet Lady G

programme.title: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical
programme.desc: Bookish wunderkind Matilda has the misfortune to be raised by garish used car salesman Mr Wormwood and his monstrous wife. The precocious youngster harnesses dormant telekinetic powers when she enrols at Crunchem Hall under hulking headmistress Agatha Trunchbull. Starring Alisha Weir and Emma Thompson

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: While on his way to the station to gather more information about his brother's last case, John finds himself drawn to another grisly crime scene at a grand Manor House but what looks to be a missing person's case turns into something far more sinister. Lucy sets out on a fact finding mission at the home of one of her husband's ex-colleagues

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: After discovering Mal is seeing the gym teacher, Amanda goes on a date with South African property developer Johannes. Things get serious between Georgie and Anne's son Darius and Felicity seems to have taken a lover of her own

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Anne organises a camping trip to the New Forest but what should be a wholesome weekend away ends up being a nightmare including snakes, drugs and UTIs. Johannes rubs Mal up the wrong way and Della has to deal with a disaster at the restaurant

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda takes over organising the end-of-season football awards, so no more tracksuits and sausage rolls. It's now black tie and cocktails. Not everyone is happy about this. Comedy, starring Lucy Punch

programme.title: Amandaland Christmas Special
programme.desc: The family heads to Aunt Joan's country house for Christmas, but things fall apart when Mal and Anne tag along and Amanda struggles to recreate the magic from her youth. Anne is in despair at being separated from her family and Felicity is increasingly irritated by her sister's high spirits, while Mal finds a hidden photo that appears to reveal a deep family secret. Jennifer Saunders guest stars in a festive episode of the Motherland spin-off, starring Lucy Punch, Joanna Lumley and Philippa Dunne

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie concocts 'an absolutely amazing' murder mystery evening, full of twists and turns. Solving the murder should be easy - providing he can keep it all on track. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The gang learns that it's Stewart's birthday. However, he doesn't want them to make a big fuss out of it in case his grandmother or colleagues find out he's gay. The queens therefore come up with a cunning plan. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Middlesbrough Pride finds Mam hosting the main stage and booking a Girls Aloud drag tribute act. the event 'promises' to be a banger Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Steph McGovern as herself. alongside Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell, Michelle Visage, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri embarks on a road trip with her mother to visit Oscar's aunt, but an accident stalls them and Caroline is forced to discuss how she truly feels about her daughter's time in prison. Meanwhile, Mandy continues her crusade to get her best friend back, but it turns out that today is the anniversary of Lara's murder. Comedy drama, starring Daisy Haggard and Geraldine James

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri sets about mending her friendship with Billy and seems to have made progress until an encounter with his wife Anna takes a dark turn and the police are called in. Having ended her relationship with Dom, Caroline is determined to relight the fire between her and Oscar. Comedy drama, starring Daisy Haggard, Geraldine James and Adeel Akhtar

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri agrees to meet the mysterious Scandinavian man who's been following her since her return and who claims to know why Lara was so angry with her on that fateful night. Miri returns home to find that the vandals have struck again and things come to a head as she confronts the community at a meeting held to run her out of town. As it erupts in turmoil, secrets and lies are laid bare, and it turns out that Miri isn't the only one with a dark past. Daisy Haggard stars, with Geraldine James and Adeel Akhtar

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: Characters in this episode include a pair of vaping dominatrixes, and the Greek-Cypriot entrepreneurs make their own James Bond movie. With Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: The Brothers Pomodoro return to show off their new cookbook and there's a song about snakes

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: The Internet Nails gang hands out special awards and there is an east London candle workshop. Sketch show, starring Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards 2023
programme.desc: The contest heads to The Braid in Ballymena, County Antrim where award-winning stand-up Shane Todd hosts with Diona Doherty, Martin Angolo and head judge Josh Pugh

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Doctor and Clara arrive at an underwater base located at the bottom of a lake. The crew have recovered a derelict spaceship from the depths, but since bringing it on board, the station has been overrun with what appear to be ghosts. As the spectral creatures prey on the crew to boost their numbers, the Doctor must determine what they really are and what they want

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The underwater base is overrun by the ghosts, while the Doctor travels back into the past to learn the origins of the derelict spaceship. Arriving in a military outpost that will soon become the lake where the station is when a dam bursts, he encounters an alien warlord making desperate plans to ensure his survival. Guest starring Morven Christie and Paul Kaye

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: It's been over a year since Frank's death and Wendy, rediscovering a long lost love for painting, decides to create a very unique tribute to him in oils. The usually curmudgeonly Geoffrey seems very keen on the commemorative artwork, but Lee and Lucy simply hate it. Once it's hanging on their wall, the pair contrive to 'lose' the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Deborah Grant and Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the silly mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she read it. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira and David try to earn some money by selling cosmetics to the locals, while Johnny heads to Elmdale in the hope of collecting unemployment insurance

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny reluctantly agrees to speak at the funeral of a local man he did not really know, while Alexis struggles with her new boyfriend's love of animals

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira go to Roland's annual Hawaiian party, Alexis and Ted host a couples' dinner party, and David and Stevie's relationship heats up

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him and he locks himself away in a cupboard, so it is up to Agnes, Father Quinn and best man Buster to try to calm him down. Meanwhile, Rory's secret is revealed to his mother. Bawdy comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Paddy Houlihan

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Worried about his dad's health, Richard battles officious receptionists - and a stubborn Ken - to get him a same-day appointment with Dr Roach. Later, Richard gets all-star struck when he learns his acting idol Lydia Bartlett is in Forres filming, while Emily suggests he imparts his wisdom to the local am-dram group. Guest starring Samantha Spiro

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard embarks on a journey to gather together some of Ken's nearest and dearest for his surprise 75th birthday party. However, tracking down the elusive Brian - Ken's oldest friend whom he lost touch with years ago - proves quite the challenge. Elsewhere, Richard's self-taped audition for a major role is troublesome and he's lost for words when Emily admits she's going on a date with a guy called Dave. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: While Richard goes to great lengths to hide the fact he's Norway-bound for his big TV break, Ken busies himself planning the special spot to scatter his beloved Jean's ashes - even if that means a less-than-sporting response from Rod at the football ground. As well as being a decoy for Richard's departure, Emily is on hand to offer advice when things go awry for the Pritchards. Comedy, starring Greg McHugh, Gregor Fisher and Amy Lennnox

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Comedy drama about a British bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, focusing on the camaraderie, bonds and banter the soldiers in unit Bluestone 42 share, despite the dangers they face in their work. The team faces a difficult roadside operation, while new padre Mary grabs the attention of expert Nick. Oliver Chris, Stephen Wight and Kelly Adams star

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick is desperate to find decent food to use in an attempt to seduce Mary. Meanwhile, Simon gets into trouble on roadside operations. Comedy drama about a British bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, starring Oliver Chris, Stephen Wright and Kelly Adams

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: The team joins a midnight raid on a bomb factory, but discover more than they expected. Meanwhile, Simon tries to get a book deal for his memoir, and an aid worker is kidnapped. Comedy drama about a bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, starring Stephen Wright and Oliver Chris

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Mary reveals a secret about her life before she was a padre - and Nick intends to take full advantage of what she discloses. Meanwhile, Bird is offered a promotion. Comedy about a British bomb disposal unit in Afghanistan, starring Kelly Adams, Oliver Chris and Katie Lyons

programme.title: World Cup 1998
programme.desc: Argentina v England. Brief highlights of the round-of-16 match from Stade Geoffroy-Guichard in Saint-Étienne, France, including a stunning solo goal and an infamous red card

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Fox-Truemans rally together, Priya questions Max's intentions, and Will tries to help Billy in his battle with Bea

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sheila attempts to win Chelsea over, Priya clashes with Cindy, and Avani grows suspicious of her mum

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max is left rattled by Priya's behaviour, Cindy and Max toast their engagement in the Vic, and Bea's influence intensifies

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea's behaviour leaves Honey concerned, Cindy clashes with Zoe over business rivalry, and Walford celebrates Pride

programme.title: Rooney
programme.desc: Documentary providing access to the life and career of former England striker and current Birmingham City manager Wayne Rooney. With guest appearances from other famous footballers, including Thierry Henry, David Beckham and Rio Ferdinand

programme.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
programme.desc: A look at the careers of Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, widely regarded as the finest players of their generation, and long-time rivals at Barcelona and Real Madrid respectively. The two players have won the Ballon d'or 13 times between them, with Messi winning eight of them, and they both head to this World Cup for what is likely to be the final time

programme.title: World Cup 2022
programme.desc: Argentina v France. Action from the 2022 World Cup final at Lusail Stadium in Qatar

programme.title: Desmond Armstrong: America's World Cup Pioneer
programme.desc: The story of the first black player to represent the United States men's national football team at a World Cup and part of the squad that qualified for Italia 90

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the world's biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs' inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: I Am Legend
programme.desc: A Manhattan-based military scientist finds himself immune to a virus that turns humans into ferocious vampire-like creatures. After the disease has swept across the world, wiping out an estimated 90 per cent of the human population, he devotes his solitary existence to finding a cure by unlocking the secret within his blood. Sci-fi thriller, starring Will Smith, Alice Braga and Charlie Tahan

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond tackle the traffic chaos snowy weather causes in Britain, and hit upon a solution involving a combine harvester, some rudimentary engineering skills and a trip to Norway. Jeremy also tests the Audi RS5 and the BMW M3, and American actress and model Amber Heard is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: World Cup 2014
programme.desc: Spain v Netherlands. Brief highlights of the Group B match at Itaipava Arena Fonte Nova in Salvador, Brazil

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: Now that Jamma's in a committed relationship, everyone starts to fancy him. However, instead of focusing on his own issues, he ends up playing therapist to his parents. At work he attempts to bond with Isaac to land the next big project. Sitcom, written by and starring Mawaan Rizwan

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: Guy is eager to meet Jamma's parents, but Jamma faces a challenge in getting his mother to agree to the introduction. He gets theatrical to try to uncover the truth behind Farida's actions - but is he prepared for what he'll discover? Russell Tovey stars

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: To outshine his brother, Jamma takes on too many work commitments. He also secures a flat with Winnie, but dreads the inevitable conversation with Guy. And with Saif mysteriously absent, Jamma embarks on a quest to find him, but as he searches for answers, he finds himself preoccupied by what lurks beneath the floorboards at the Megacentre

programme.title: Juice
programme.desc: Jamma and his colleagues embark on an escape room adventure to foster team bonding after a disastrous pitch. Jamma tries to keep Winnie and Guy from talking to each other in hopes of covering up his lie, but instead finds himself entering a gateway into the past. Comedy, starring Mawaan Rizwan

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts help more families declutter their homes, beginning with Tom and Chloe Watling and their two daughters. All their possessions are packed up and laid out in a giant warehouse, including 459 books and magazines, 326 pieces of baking equipment, 39 unopened travel-sized jars of jam and 1,672 toys. Back at the house, carpenter Rob sorts out Tom's new office and the girls' playroom, cleaner Iwan gives the home a supersize spring clean, and Dilly meticulously organises their extended kitchen and resurrects the neglected bakery

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In the waters off the coast of Borneo, Steve Backshall comes face to face with a venomous scorpion fish and witnesses the spear-mantis impaling its supper

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of a Jaguar XJ and races against the rotation of the Earth. James May travels to America to drive a Nasa Space Exploration Vehicle and Richard Hammond tests two of the most famous supercars of the 1980s - the Porsche 959 and the Ferrari F40. John Prescott is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: England 2006: The Golden Generation
programme.desc: Documentary exploring why the men's England football team did not fulfil their potential at the 2006 FIFA World Cup. Sven-Goran Eriksson's talented side was captained by the talismanic David Beckham, but the players and their partners came under increasing scrutiny off the pitch, and they were eventually eliminated by Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal. With contributions from their key members of the squad, including Rio Ferdinand, Wayne Rooney, Ashley Cole, Owen Hargreaves and Paul Robinson

programme.title: Rooney
programme.desc: Documentary examining the life and career of Wayne Rooney, from his professional debut at the age of 16 with Everton to his goal-scoring exploits with Manchester United and England

programme.title: David Brent: Life on the Road
programme.desc: More than a decade on from his appearance in documentary series The Office, a film crew is once again following the life of David Brent. He is now working as a rep for a cleaning-goods distributor, but harbours dreams of making it big in the music business. Acting on this, he takes three weeks off work and tours with a new-look version of Foregone Conclusion, the band he fronted in the 1990s. Mockumentary comedy, starring Ricky Gervais, Ben Bailey Smith and Tom Basden

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: World Cup 2018
programme.desc: France v Croatia. Action from the final at Luzhniki Stadium in Moscow

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of a Jaguar XJ and races against the rotation of the Earth. James May travels to America to drive a Nasa Space Exploration Vehicle and Richard Hammond tests two of the most famous supercars of the 1980s - the Porsche 959 and the Ferrari F40. John Prescott is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Dance Race
programme.desc: New Zealand choreographer Corey Baker's short film which a range of dancers, from ballet and Strictly stars to choreographed skydivers and drivers, race across the UK to meet Lady G

programme.title: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical
programme.desc: Bookish wunderkind Matilda has the misfortune to be raised by garish used car salesman Mr Wormwood and his monstrous wife. The precocious youngster harnesses dormant telekinetic powers when she enrols at Crunchem Hall under hulking headmistress Agatha Trunchbull. Starring Alisha Weir and Emma Thompson

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: While on his way to the station to gather more information about his brother's last case, John finds himself drawn to another grisly crime scene at a grand Manor House but what looks to be a missing person's case turns into something far more sinister. Lucy sets out on a fact finding mission at the home of one of her husband's ex-colleagues

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: After discovering Mal is seeing the gym teacher, Amanda goes on a date with South African property developer Johannes. Things get serious between Georgie and Anne's son Darius and Felicity seems to have taken a lover of her own

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Anne organises a camping trip to the New Forest but what should be a wholesome weekend away ends up being a nightmare including snakes, drugs and UTIs. Johannes rubs Mal up the wrong way and Della has to deal with a disaster at the restaurant

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda takes over organising the end-of-season football awards, so no more tracksuits and sausage rolls. It's now black tie and cocktails. Not everyone is happy about this. Comedy, starring Lucy Punch

programme.title: Amandaland Christmas Special
programme.desc: The family heads to Aunt Joan's country house for Christmas, but things fall apart when Mal and Anne tag along and Amanda struggles to recreate the magic from her youth. Anne is in despair at being separated from her family and Felicity is increasingly irritated by her sister's high spirits, while Mal finds a hidden photo that appears to reveal a deep family secret. Jennifer Saunders guest stars in a festive episode of the Motherland spin-off, starring Lucy Punch, Joanna Lumley and Philippa Dunne

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie concocts 'an absolutely amazing' murder mystery evening, full of twists and turns. Solving the murder should be easy - providing he can keep it all on track. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The gang learns that it's Stewart's birthday. However, he doesn't want them to make a big fuss out of it in case his grandmother or colleagues find out he's gay. The queens therefore come up with a cunning plan. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Middlesbrough Pride finds Mam hosting the main stage and booking a Girls Aloud drag tribute act. the event 'promises' to be a banger Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Steph McGovern as herself. alongside Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell, Michelle Visage, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri embarks on a road trip with her mother to visit Oscar's aunt, but an accident stalls them and Caroline is forced to discuss how she truly feels about her daughter's time in prison. Meanwhile, Mandy continues her crusade to get her best friend back, but it turns out that today is the anniversary of Lara's murder. Comedy drama, starring Daisy Haggard and Geraldine James

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri sets about mending her friendship with Billy and seems to have made progress until an encounter with his wife Anna takes a dark turn and the police are called in. Having ended her relationship with Dom, Caroline is determined to relight the fire between her and Oscar. Comedy drama, starring Daisy Haggard, Geraldine James and Adeel Akhtar

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri agrees to meet the mysterious Scandinavian man who's been following her since her return and who claims to know why Lara was so angry with her on that fateful night. Miri returns home to find that the vandals have struck again and things come to a head as she confronts the community at a meeting held to run her out of town. As it erupts in turmoil, secrets and lies are laid bare, and it turns out that Miri isn't the only one with a dark past. Daisy Haggard stars, with Geraldine James and Adeel Akhtar

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: Characters in this episode include a pair of vaping dominatrixes, and the Greek-Cypriot entrepreneurs make their own James Bond movie. With Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: The Brothers Pomodoro return to show off their new cookbook and there's a song about snakes

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia
programme.desc: The Internet Nails gang hands out special awards and there is an east London candle workshop. Sketch show, starring Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou

programme.desc: The contest heads to The Braid in Ballymena, County Antrim where award-winning stand-up Shane Todd hosts with Diona Doherty, Martin Angolo and head judge Josh Pugh

programme.desc: Cosima is left in charge for a day and accidentally hosts a massive party

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Spicy directs a show starring Sunny and the eaglets and Weasy wants to join in, but Spicy worries he will overdo it and refuses to give him a role

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Forest inspectors pay a visit to check its super-happy status and Friendlyface sends the heroes on outlandish quests to guarantee results

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play with a baby ostrich that Grizzy found outside

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: There is chaos in a toilet roll factory when Duck winds up looking like a spooky mummy

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons: Rising Hope
programme.desc: The Rising Volt Tacklers reunite and prepare to take to the skies once more

programme.title: Winx Club - The Magic Is Back
programme.desc: Bloom is torn when she learns she must take a test at Alfea to determine whether she is a fairy or a witch, and the Trix take advantage of her doubts, pushing her closer to despair

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Every single Gladiator gets revenge on Nitro, who thinks he is filming a science show, but phony interruptions and bogus camera hiccups lead to an epic pie pandemonium finale

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Joel Mawhinney takes on his final challenge

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
programme.desc: One-inch-tall, googly-eyed talking shell Marcel lives with his grandmother Nana Connie, who is in the early stages of dementia. Filmmaker Dean rents the house and becomes fascinated by the day-to-day activities of his tiny housemate. Animated comedy, with the voices of Dean Fleischer Camp, Jenny Slate and Isabella Rossellini

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost works on his new garden by planting shrubs and building a path, creating a potted herb garden, and suggesting wall plants for sun or shade. In Devon, Carol Klein combines flowers and edibles, and at West Dean Gardens, Jamie Butterworth shares delphinium-growing tips. Plus, inspiring rose and cottage gardens are showcased in Birmingham and Norwich

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents coverage of the opening play on day six of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, including third-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Clare Tauson was among the early winners on the sixth day 12 months ago, overcoming 2022 title-winner Elena Rybakina 7-6, 6-3, before defending champion Barbora Krejcíková also fell in a 2-6, 6-3, 6-4 defeat at the hands of Emma Navarro. Over on Centre Court, the player who eventually succeeded Krejcíková, Iga Swiatek, was in good form as she won 6-2, 6-3 against Danielle Collins

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Further coverage of day six of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, where third-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions are scheduled to conclude. Grigor Dimitrov overcame Sebastian Ofner 6-3, 6-4, 7-6 at this stage in 2025, and despite slipping down the rankings since due to injury, he returned again this year after being given a wild card. Tenth seed Ben Shelton and lucky loser Márton Fucsovics were last up on Court No. 1 last year, and it was Shelton who prevailed 6-3, 7-6, 6-2. Having previously reached the semi-finals of both the Australian and US Opens, the American will have gone into the tournament with high hopes of reaching his maiden Grand Slam final this time around

programme.title: 50 American Music Icons at the BBC
programme.desc: To mark the United States celebrating 250 years of Independence, this programme features 50 of the biggest names in modern American music and their performances over the years at the BBC. It's a playlist of acts covering 70 years of classic hits, that includes the most influential and successful artists, bands and songwriters who made the journey from the US charts to international superstardom, and on their way also took time to appear on a UK audiences' TV screens. Featuring performances by Beyoncé, Bruce Springsteen, Ella Fitzgerald, Nirvana, Liberace, Johnny Cash, Louis Armstrong, Lady Gaga, Carole King, Bruno Marsand Tina Turner

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Highlights of the sixth day's play at the All England Club, which was scheduled to feature matches in the third round of the men's and ladies' singles

programme.title: Cricket: T20 Highlights
programme.desc: England v India. Highlights of the second T20 in the five-match series from Emirates Old Trafford, Manchester

programme.title: Godland
programme.desc: A young Danish priest is sent to late 19th-century Iceland with a mission to set up a parish in a remote part of the country. Greeted with an ambivalent reception by less-than-welcoming locals, his zeal, faith and sense of reality start to drift as he ventures deeper into the Icelandic landscape. Drama, starring Ingvar Sigurdsson. In Danish and Icelandic

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and TV presenter Joe Swash traces the history of his father's family, who he lost at the age of 12, knowing only that they supposedly came from Italy. Using documents given to him by his mother and the help of experts, he discovers stories of murder, banditry, a political activist and a family bar in southern Italy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden visits Bodnant Garden in Snowdonia, where she explores an area reminiscent of a Himalayan valley amid the mountains of north Wales. An unexpected view at the nearby Bodysgallen Hall takes Christine back to her childhood holidays, and she also hears the story of how the Snowdon mountain railway was built

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley mark the 50th anniversary of the Viking Way. They uncover evidence of Viking settlers, meet the volunteers who maintain the route, visit the Ramblers' Church, and investigate efforts to protect water voles. Adam Henson explores high-tech strawberry farming, and John Craven launches Countryfile's annual photographic competition.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Lizzie Schofield and Brian Cunningham start new projects and check on the progress of planting carried out in previous years. Lizzie is in the herb garden that she renovated in 2024, assessing what has worked and what needs refreshed, while Brian checks on the apple trees he planted in the spring as examples of espalier shaping and pruning. Plus, a look at what plants are good for attracting birds to gardens

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and the team are at the Royal Highland Show, where Cammy Wilson catches up with a sheepdresser making finishing touches before showing his Bleu du Maines. Rosie Morton gets a taste of Aberdeenshire in the food and drink hall showcasing artisan producers, while Arlene Stuart finds the out about the complexities of judging honey. As Landward celebrates 50 years on air, Dougie looks back on show coverage from the archives

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A naive young woman becomes governess in a mysterious Yorkshire mansion and before long she is completely besotted with her charismatic but intimidating employer. But unbeknown to her, the master of the house is concealing a dark secret. Romantic period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Joan Fontaine, Orson Welles, Agnes Moorehead and Margaret O'Brien

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Laura Robson presents coverage of the opening play on day seven of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, where the fourth round is scheduled to get under way in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Seventeenth seed Karen Khachanov was the first player to secure a place in the quarter-finals last year, overcoming Kamil Majchrzak 6-4, 6-2, 6-3. He was followed onto the No 2 Court by Laura Siegemund, who was equally comfortable in a 6-3, 6-2 victory over Solana Sierra

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Further coverage of day seven of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, featuring fourth-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Top seed Aryna Sabalenka claimed a 6-4, 7-6 victory over Elise Mertens at this stage last year. A fourth Grand Slam title and first at Wimbledon would eventually elude her, but she did go on to win another major later in the year when she claimed the US Open title for the second time, and given she is still top of the world rankings, it would be a surprise if she wasn't still involved at this stage yet again

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani begins a four-part journey along England's east coast, exploring the wildlife, landscapes and communities adapting to life on the edge of the sea. Her first stop is Northey Island in Essex, an internationally important refuge for threatened birds including curlew, lapwing and dunlin. With rising sea levels putting increasing pressure on coastal habitats, Anita finds out about an innovative conservation project using retired Thames barges to help protect the island's future, and meets volunteers working to safeguard vulnerable ringed plover nests using a simple but effective solution. Plus, Anita delves into the Countryfile archives to revisit some of the projects helping create more space for nature to thrive

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce visits Powis Castle, one of the grandest houses in Wales, where Duncan Campbell discovers a vanishingly rare silver tobacco box from the 1600s, and Serhat Ahmet examines a porcelain egg with a royal connection. Paul Atterbury is taken back to the earliest days of Dr Who, while Alexandra Aguilar values a jade ornament bought at a car boot sale for 50 pence and Geoffrey Munn uncovers a jewellery collection 'of the highest quality' with rubies and diamonds dating from the 1700s

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti travel to Turkey to work at Argos in Cappadocia, a hotel built into a network of ancient caves. Combining history with modern luxury, Monica finds herself brushing cave ceilings with housekeeping before flying above in a hot air balloon, while Rob helps with the finishing touches of a new spa. With each room providing surprises and inviting curiosity, Rob seeks further education on the history of the caves as Monica practises centuries-old traditional cooking techniques

programme.title: Murder Case: Who Killed Kiesha?
programme.desc: Detectives head to the market town of Elgin in November 2023 to investigate the murder of 32-year-old mother-of-two Kiesha Donaghy, whose body was discovered in her home and who had been the victim of a frenzied attack. As the officers search for key evidence and piece together information to uncover the truth, the case quickly unfolds into a complex investigation that demands relentless focus and all of the team's expertise

programme.title: Blade Runner
programme.desc: A former cop in a bleak future Los Angeles is forced to return to duty for a mission to hunt down and kill a group of renegade android replicants. The fugitives have escaped from a space colony to find their creator on Earth, in a bid to prolong their limited lifespan. Theatrical cut of Ridley Scott's sci-fi thriller, originally released in cinemas, starring Harrison Ford, Rutger Hauer, Sean Young and Daryl Hannah

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Highlights of the seventh day's play at the All England Club, featuring matches in the fourth round of the men's and ladies' singles

programme.title: Teddy Pendergrass: If You Don't Know Me
programme.desc: Documentary about the life, music and towering achievements of soul singer Teddy Pendergrass, from his origins and early life in Philadelphia. The film charts his early successes with Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes and his solo superstardom and sex symbol status, before moving on to his later life and continued success after overcoming the car crash that left him paralysed

programme.title: Madonna & Graham
programme.desc: Graham Norton sits down with singer-songwriter, record producer, actress, entrepreneur and charity founder Madonna. The pair discuss her lengthy career in which she has achieved 18 multi-platinum albums globally, seven Grammy Awards, two Golden Globe Awards, 20 MTV Video Music Awards and an induction to the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame

programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Ardingly antiques fair in West Sussex, and challenges punters with a mystery item. Kate Bliss and Jonathan Pratt assist the teams

programme.title: MOTD: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Mexico v England. Another chance to see the fourth round-of-16 match, which took place at Mexico City Stadium

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents coverage of the opening play on day eight from the All England Club, featuring fourth-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Flavio Cobolli was among the first people to claim victory on day eight 12 months ago, overcoming 2017 finalist Marin Cilic 6-4, 6-4, 6-7, 7-6. That contest on the No. 2 Court was followed by a 7-5, 7-5 victory for Liudmila Samsonova over Jéssica Bouzas Maneiro, which saw her reach her maiden Grand Slam singles quarter-final

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents further coverage of day eight in the Grand Slam tournament at All England Club, featuring fourth-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Seventh seed Mirra Andreeva impressed on Centre Court at this stage last year in a 6-2, 6-3 victory over Emma Navarro. Over on the No.1 Court, Ben Shelton came back from a set down to defeat Lorenzo Sonego, before eventual champion Iga Swiatek comfortably prevailed 6-4, 6-1 against Clara Tauson

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents the final coverage on day eight in the Grand Slam tournament at the All England Club, where the fourth-round matches are scheduled to conclude in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Eventual champion Jannik Sinner had a stroke of luck at this stage last year on Centre Court when Grigor Dimitrov retired hurt while 6-3, 7-5, 2-2 up against him. From there Sinner did not drop another set until the final, and he went into this year's competition as a hot favourite to win the title once again, especially given Carlos Alcaraz's absence due to injury

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse fish for wild carp in Mid Wales, spending their first day at an ancient glacial lake where Bob tries out new gear he is keen to use and hopes will make a difference to his fishing. During this trip, Bob and Paul meet Mick May and Marina Gibson, who together have started a charity called Cancer and Pisces, which offers fishing as therapeutic respite for people with cancer

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Highlights of the eighth day's play at the All England Club, featuring men's and ladies' matches in the fourth round of the singles

programme.title: Official Secrets
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the publication of a leaked GCHQ memo in the run-up to the Iraq war. The leak had devastating consequences for whistleblower Katharine Gun, who was put on trial for breaching the Official Secrets Act, and the journalists who were determined to unmask a conspiracy. Starring Keira Knightley and Matt Smith

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley mark the 50th anniversary of the Viking Way. They uncover evidence of Viking settlers, meet the volunteers who maintain the route, visit the Ramblers' Church, and investigate efforts to protect water voles. Adam Henson explores high-tech strawberry farming, and John Craven launches Countryfile's annual photographic competition.

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan celebrate the season's standout wildlife moments. Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the seabird colonies at Bempton Cliffs, where thousands of gannets, guillemots and puffins crowd the towering coastline

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress as she trains as a carer at one of the UK's leading health and social care colleges, where she will study, train, and work alongside students, care professionals, volunteers and home carers. Nerves hit on day one, but her first on-the-job work placement has a real impact

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: The stories of major crimes that were solved with the help of forensic evidence provided by expert witnesses. A ballistics expert proves the shooting of a young woman was murder not manslaughter, and a forensic scientist secures justice for a cold case victim, 20 years after the crime

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Ben Cooper help two teams search an antiques fair in Exeter for valuable items to sell at auction. Father and daughter David and Ellie's eyes are caught by a pair of etched, cut-glass decanters while partners Darren and Simon have their eyes on a stall of shiny costume jewellery. Christina Trevanion keeps fairgoers guessing with a mystery object

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Police uncover shocking evidence of animal cruelty linked to a series of farmland poaching attacks in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, and the RAF tackle a challenging rescue when Beauty the cow becomes trapped at the bottom of a steep gorge in Gilsland, Cumbria. Paul Colgan joins a rural crime officer using forensic DNA technology to mark valuable machinery in Ellerton, East Yorkshire

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The freedivers and scuba team join forces to try to find a camera filled with precious memories at the bottom of Grasmere. Archive detective Sam is challenged to locate the owner of a First World War medal found by local detectorists. Ceramicist Lucy turns items fished from the lake into stunning works of art. Presented by Helen Skelton
