programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents coverage of the opening play on day nine of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, featuring various doubles matches and the start of the men's and ladies' singles quarter-finals. Aryna Sabalenka dropped the first set to veteran Laura Siegemund at this stage last year, but then showed why she is the world's top-ranked player by recovering to win 4-6, 6-2, 6-4

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Further coverage of day nine of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, including the men's and ladies' singles quarter-finals. Amanda Anisimova impressed on the No. 1 Court at this stage last year with a 6-1, 7-6 victory over Anastasia Pavlyuchenkova. The American would eventually finish the tournament as runner-up to Iga Swiatek and she was also second to Aryna Sabalenka in the 2025 US Open, so will have gone into this event determined to go one better and win her maiden Grand Slam title

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson demonstrates how to prepare a range of straightforward yet sumptuous Italian dishes, including her recipe for roast chicken with saffron orzotto. She also creates two desserts - coffee ice-cream and a chocolate olive-oil cake - and a pasta dish with courgettes that is ideal for late-night snacking

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea completely unravels, and Avani tries to reach out to Ravi

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits more archaeological sites around the country, with digs in southern England revealing a previously unknown Roman town, a Tudor ship buried beneath a quarry and evidence of Henry VIII's financial forgery under the Tower of London

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Something is holding Mandy back and she needs to find out what it is, whether she can overcome it and if there is anything she can do about it. Comedy, written by and starring Diane Morgan

programme.title: Cricket: T20 Highlights
programme.desc: England v India. Action from the third T20 in the five-match series, which took place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham

programme.title: The Farthest: Voyager's Interstellar Journey
programme.desc: The story of the twin Voyager probes, launched 16 days apart in 1977 and which continue to beam back revolutionary information across unimaginable distances. This film tells the tales of these machines, the men and women who built them, and the vision that propelled them farther than anyone could ever have hoped

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan are bidding farewell to the nests they've followed throughout the series at National Trust Crom in Northern Ireland. They reflect on the unforgettable wildlife stories that have defined the spring of 2026

programme.title: Britney: In Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Britney Spears was one of the biggest pop stars of her generation, and like many music stars, she was defined by imagery. But she was also the subject of extraordinary exploitation and control. Years of personal and professional turmoil would play out in the frenzied glare of the media spotlight. With the aid of 10 photos, this is the story of a manufactured child star fighting for control over her music, her life and, ultimately, her image

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A project in the West Midlands that has taken over six years finally comes to an end and Martel Maxwell visits a property with potential in Livingston. Dion Dublin is impressed by a spacious property on offer in Leeds

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion goes on the trail of scientific treasures near Swindon, while Ben Cooper and Gary Pe help the teams at an antiques fair in Devon

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in the home of Japanese porcelain, Arita, where a railway line runs straight through the town's stunning porcelain Shinto shrine. He also stays in a traditional hotel with paper walls and tatami mats where he enjoys a relaxing soak in a natural thermal bath. Michael ends this leg in Fukuoka, Japan's fasting-growing city, full of young Japanese who flock there for the food culture and entrepreneurial spirit

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour through Uzbekistan continues in the lush oasis surrounding the historic city of Samarkand. Here, he uncovers the Central Asian origins of the apple, a fruit that travelled to Europe along the Silk Road. Boarding a high-speed train along the old Trans-Caspian Railway, Michael also hears how the Russians toiled to build this remarkable line through the shifting sands of Uzbekistan's steppe

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents coverage of day 10 of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, as the quarter-finals continue in the men's and women's singles competitions. Iga Swiatek was the first player to advance to the last four on day 10 in 2025, claiming a 6-2, 7-5 victory over Liudmila Samsonova, and she went on to win this title for the first time in her career

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents further coverage of day 10 of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, including more men's and ladies' singles quarter-final action, plus doubles and wheelchair matches. Jannik Sinner's road to the title continued at this stage last year with a 7-6, 6-4, 6-4 victory over Ben Shelton. Elsewhere, top ladies' doubles seeds Katerina Siniaková and Taylor Townsend won 6-6, 6-3 against Tímea Babos and Luisa Stefani

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson explores St Margaret's on the Kent coast, learning how an exclusive retreat for the rich and famous suddenly became known as Hellfire Corner during the Second World War. Ben examines graffiti in a visit to a secret air-raid shelter and tours the site of Britain's biggest guns, while on the beach the archaeologist runs his eye over the hideaway lived in by Noel Coward and his partner after the war

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya finds herself trapped, Jack pushes Kim to visit Denise, and Kojo asks Harry and Gina to help him find a girlfriend

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts restore a portable drum kit from the 1940s, an oil painting thought to be 175 years old, a patchwork toy hippopotamus and a pair of baby shoes given to a soldier by the family of a little girl he rescued in the Netherlands during the Second World War

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their country's current crises

programme.title: Mackenzie Crook and Martin Freeman Remember The Office
programme.desc: Mackenzie Crook and Martin Freeman look back on UK comedy series The Office, in which they portrayed the characters Gareth and Tim. They share their memories of working and laughing, alongside Ricky Gervais, co-writer Stephen Merchant, and their other leading office colleague Lucy Davis, who played the object of Tim's affections, Dawn

programme.title: My Summer of Love
programme.desc: Disillusioned working-class teenager Mona forms a close friendship with Tamsin, a wealthy girl neglected by her parents and troubled by her sister's death. Their bond develops into an intense, secret affair, provoking a violent reaction in Mona's devoutly religious ex-convict brother. Drama based on Helen Cross's novel, starring Nathalie Press, Emily Blunt and Paddy Considine

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook on their knowledge of Paisley, in the west central Lowlands of Scotland

programme.title: What Happened at the Somme
programme.desc: Journalist Jordan Dunbar marks the 110th anniversary of the Battle of the Somme by speaking to those who are still discovering the truth of what took place through returned medals, photographs that have been kept hidden for years and names not spoken in long silenced family stories. He uncovers the century-long shadow that the battle has cast over families across the UK in Belfast, Lancashire and London along with a new insight into his own family history

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: After 18 months behind bars, Sean returns to his community in Cambuslang, where he struggles to manage his ADHD, battles with addiction and faces a court case for armed robbery. Self-styled vigilante Frankie lives by his own brutal code of jail justice in which he attacks sex offenders, while violent offender and drug user Owen attempts to rebuild his life on the outside - until his violent past catches up with him

Anastasia Pavlyuchenkova was one of the first players to book her place in round four last year when she prevailed 3-6, 6-4, 6-4 against four-time Grand Slam-winner Naomi Osaka. Also progressing was Great Britain's Sonay Kartal, who comfortably defeated French qualifier Diane Parry 6-4, 6-2, having never previously been beyond the second round of a major championship

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents further coverage of day five from the All England Club, where third-round matches are scheduled to continue in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Elsewhere there was a shock when Laura Siegemund prevailed 6-3, 6-3 against 2025 Australian Open champion and sixth seed Madison Keys, and Karen Khachanov came through an attritional five-set affair with Nuno Borges

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents the concluding coverage of day five from the All England Club, featuring third-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are admiring everything Duggee has grown in his vegetable garden, especially his crop of giant carrots, when they hear a noise. They investigate and find the Rabbits busy preparing for the Carrot Solstice, the day when the sun is at its highest. On this day, they gather round Carrot Henge to celebrate the glory of this humble vegetable. Trouble is, the Rabbits need to go and get ready - but who will look after the carrots? The Squirrels volunteer, so off the Rabbits go. Looking after the carrots seems easy at first, and Norrie arranges tasks for everyone. Betty skillfully organises the queue while Roly is on toilet duty. And then, disaster strikes. Worm pops up and eats part of Carrot Henge. Duggee is dispatched to fetch one of his own giant carrots to fill the gap. As time ticks on with no Duggee in sight, the place gets busier, and the sun is nearly at the top. Will Duggee fix the Henge in time, or will Carrot Solstice be ruined?

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome Zuri, Otis, Theo, Reau, Elijah and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Giovanna has a tambourine inside her bag. All the parents and babies have different noisy objects and toys in their bags too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are encouraged to hide baby-appropriate noisy toys and objects inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, and then play with the items together! The fun children's song Noisy Noisy Shaker features, as does a story about Baby Bear and Baby Tiger making lots of noise, and viewers at home show how they've been exploring sounds too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

Rory and his son Sandy are on Longniddry Beach looking in rockpools for starfish and seastars. Chantelle and Rory's fact files explain about the shape of starfish, how long they can live and how starfish stop other creatures eating them. It's also time to find out why they are an important keystone species. This episode's Teeny Tiny Task is to help the beach and the creatures by litter picking

Diesel is trained to listen out for important sounds that Rayane might miss. Diesel is Rayane's ears. Together, they are unstoppable! Rayane is playing the detective in the drama club play, and Diesel is going to be right by his side as his trusty police dog. At the theatre, Diesel helps Rayane and his friend Grace practise for something called a 'cue' - their big moment to go on stage. Diesel must give Rayane a nudge when he hears two rings from a big red telephone. But during the show, someone's phone goes off in the audience! Can Diesel save the day and make sure that Rayane takes to the stage at the right time?

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Theo, River, Arthur, Reece, Luna and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Giovanna has got a blue bib and blue sunglasses inside her bag. All the parents and babies have different blue toys and objects in their bags too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are encouraged to hide a safe, baby-appropriate blue object or toy inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, and then play with the items together! The traditional nursery rhyme Little Boy Blue features, as does a story about Baby Bear and Baby Tiger dressing up in blue things, and viewers at home show how they've been exploring the colour blue too. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory scarves and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are admiring everything Duggee has grown in his vegetable garden, especially his crop of giant carrots, when they hear a noise. They investigate and find the Rabbits busy preparing for the Carrot Solstice, the day when the sun is at its highest. On this day, they gather round Carrot Henge to celebrate the glory of this humble vegetable. Trouble is, the Rabbits need to go and get ready - but who will look after the carrots? The Squirrels volunteer, so off the Rabbits go. Looking after the carrots seems easy at first, and Norrie arranges tasks for everyone. Betty skillfully organises the queue while Roly is on toilet duty. And then, disaster strikes. Worm pops up and eats part of Carrot Henge. Duggee is dispatched to fetch one of his own giant carrots to fill the gap. As time ticks on with no Duggee in sight, the place gets busier, and the sun is nearly at the top. Will Duggee fix the Henge in time, or will Carrot Solstice be ruined?

All the parents and babies have different noisy objects and toys in their bags too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are encouraged to hide baby-appropriate noisy toys and objects inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, and then play with the items together! The fun children's song Noisy Noisy Shaker features, as does a story about Baby Bear and Baby Tiger making lots of noise, and viewers at home show how they've been exploring sounds too. Chantelle and Rory's fact files explain about the shape of starfish, how long they can live and how starfish stop other creatures eating them. Rayane is playing the detective in the drama club play, and Diesel is going to be right by his side as his trusty police dog. At the theatre, Diesel helps Rayane and his friend Grace practise for something called a 'cue' - their big moment to go on stage. Diesel must give Rayane a nudge when he hears two rings from a big red telephone. But during the show, someone's phone goes off in the audience! Along the way, he befriends one of the creatures and gets caught up in chase along the salt flats as they dodge the giant legs and long necks of a herd of diplodocus

programme.title: Molly and Mack

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Cricketer Abtaha Maqsood reads Not Now, Noor!, which tells the tale of a curious little girl who is trying to find out why her grown-ups wear hijabs

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The next leg of his trip sees James heading to Bresse, home of one of the country's most famous ingredients - the Bresse chicken. He visits a chicken farm to discover just why these birds are so special, and gets the chance to cook his own. He also meets Georges Blanc, one of his culinary heroes, before attending the annual Michelin star football tournament

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: The chef meets a family who live off grid, samples fabada stew, Urbies cheese, and visits a restaurant famous for its sardines

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: New Zealand v France (Kick-off 8.10am). Ugo Monye presents all the action from the opening round-robin match of the inaugural rugby union competition, held at One New Zealand Stadium. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi and Sean Fitzpatrick, and commentary by Gareth Rhys Owen and Benjamin Kayser

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: Australia v Ireland (Kick-off 11.10am). Ugo Monye presents coverage of the rugby union match at Allianz Stadium in Sydney. Wallabies coach Joe Schmidt will have fond memories of his time in Ireland - he enjoyed a successful six year spell as head coach. His counterpart Andy Farrell has just signed a long term deal to lead the Irish team and will be hoping to build on a largely positive Six Nations campaign which saw them finish second to France. With analysis from Rory Best and George Gregan, and commentary by Martin Gillingham and Gordon D'Arcy

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: Fiji v Wales (Kick-off 2.10pm). Lauren Salter presents all the action from the rugby union match at Cardiff City Stadium. While Fiji are the designated hosts, the match is being played here because their main stadium does not meet the competition requirements, and Wales will be looking to take advantage of that and build on their long-awaited Six Nations victory over Italy in March. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Jamie Roberts and Dan Biggar, and commentary by Miles Harrison, Shane Williams and Ben Kay

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: ITV News and Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Canada v Morocco
programme.desc: Canada v Morocco (Kick-off 6.00pm). Coverage of the first round-of-16 match, which takes place at Houston Stadium in the USA. Canada edged past South Africa 1-0 in the last round, in a match that is unlikely to live long in the memory, but the Canadians won't let that bother them, although they'll be underdogs this time against a side looking to emulate their achievements of four years ago, when they reached the semi-finals. Morocco come into this match on the back of a penalty shootout victory over the Netherlands, secured thanks to a late equaliser from Fulham defender Issa Diop

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: Comedian Tom Allen celebrates the chaos that occurs when making TV shows with unseen outtakes from dramas, comedies and entertainment shows. This episode features an accidental innuendo with Idris Elba that leaves Lorraine in tears of laughter, Love Island filming that is interrupted by angry animals, and an interview with Hollywood star Paul Rudd that ends in disaster

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: ITV News and Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Dirty Dancing
programme.desc: A dance instructor at a 1960s resort hotel fears his career will be jeopardised when his regular partner is forced to pull out of an important show in which they are due to perform. Luckily, a naive teenager is happy to step in, and is treated to a whirlwind holiday education in life, love and expressing herself. Romantic drama, with Patrick Swayze, Jennifer Grey and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: The chef discovers how migas is made in Extremadura, meets an award-winning tortilla chef, and visits a local brewery. He also creates a garlic and bean stew, poussin, and a cherry beer and chocolate cake

He is also joined by Ruby Bhogal, who tries to find out the secret behind the best fried chicken, and actress Sheree Murphy, who makes chicken pot pie

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to Normandy and sets up his mobile kitchen in one of the many calvados orchards in the area, making a dish of artichokes with hollandaise. He also visits restaurant Le Pavé d'Auge in Beuvron-en-Auge to meets a chef who likes to cook with as much butter as he does

programme.title: Thunderball
programme.desc: James Bond travels to the Bahamas on the trail of two nuclear warheads stolen from a British aircraft. The lethal weapons have fallen into the hands of international crime syndicate Spectre, which plans to fire them at the US coast unless an exorbitant ransom is paid. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Claudine Auger, Adolfo Celi and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan is joined by star of Gavin & Stacey Joanna Page, sports presenter and host of The High Performance Podcast Jake Humphrey, and India Hicks. Farmer Tom Davis brings along a pair of piglets to hog the limelight, and drinks expert Andy Clarke shares his favourite tipples inspired by the movies in the Best of British feature

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes Ellie Simmonds back for a second visit to her kitchen after the two got on so well the first time, with the five-time Paralympic swimming gold medallist baking a coffee and walnut cake. Prue's food hero is a tea producer from the Vale of Glamorgan who makes loose leaf tea and kombucha tea, while he has a versatile baked feta, roasted tomatoes and garlic dish on the menu and is pleased when a hack for egg yolks goes well

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team of gardening experts head to Waltham Abbey in Essex to create a tropical escape with a yoga deck and home office at its heart for Venessa Taylor, a selfless leukaemia survivor who campaigns to raise the awareness of blood cancer and the importance of bone marrow donations across all ethnic groups

programme.title: ITV News and Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a member of a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly

programme.title: You Only Live Twice
programme.desc: James Bond is sent on a mission to uncover the truth behind the disappearances of Soviet and American space capsules before the mutually suspicious superpowers go to war. The trail leads to Japan, where Bond finally comes face to face with his arch-enemy Blofeld. Spy thriller, starring Sean Connery, Donald Pleasence, Akiko Wakabayashi and Bernard Lee

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Brazil v Norway
programme.desc: Brazil v Norway (Kick-off 9.00pm). Coverage of the third round-of-16 match, which takes place at New York/New Jersey Stadium in the USA. Brazil came from behind to beat Japan 2-1 in injury-time, having trailed at the break, with goals by Casemiro and Gabriel Martinelli breaking Japanese hearts. Despite not quite reaching their peak yet, Brazil will start as favourites here as they look to move a step closer towards a first World Cup triumph since 2002. Norway had Erling Braut Haaland to thank for a late winner in their last-32 match against Ivory Coast, after Amad Diallo's stunning goal had seemed enough to take the game into extra time

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Coverage of the seventh round-of-16 match (Kick-off 5.00pm), which takes place at Atlanta Stadium in the USA. The fans in attendance will hope for a more entertaining affair than in the corresponding clash in Al Rayyan four years ago, as Morocco and Spain played out a monotonous 0-0 draw over 120 minutes, in which the Spanish dominated possession but did little with it as few chances were created. The tie came to life in the subsequent penalty shootout as the 2010 champions failed to convert any of their three attempts with goalkeeper Yassine Bounou saving from Sergio Busquets and Carlos Soler after Pablo Sarabia first hit the post, while the Atlas Lions scored three of their four penalties to progress. By missing their three spot kicks, Spain became only the second team to not score in a World Cup penalty shoot-out, following in the footsteps of Switzerland who lost to Ukraine in 2006 by the same 3-0 scoreline

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Switzerland v Colombia or Ghana (Kick-off 9.00pm). Coverage of the eighth round-of-16 match, which takes place at BC Place Vancouver in Canada. Switzerland had a game to forget at the corresponding stage of the 2022 World Cup when they were thrashed 6-1 by Portugal. However, they will be hopeful of a much stronger showing this time around, having topped a group containing Canada, Bosnia and Herzegovina and Qatar, before easing past Algeria 2-0 in the round of 32

There are probing questions about love, identity and belief, alongside moments of pure mischief and laughter - including an explosive question about his sexual preferences

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team of antiques experts visit Oldham, Greater Manchester, where Simon Schneider falls for a stunning clock, Michael Hogben splashes out big for some gold, and the Duke himself gets stumped by an artist's print

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show as contestant Becca tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Calum from Glasgow, Ann from Rochdale, Ali from London and Rio from Guildford pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: ITV Evening News; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain warns Graham and the rest of Tates to stay away from Kyle, while Kev tells Charles he fears that Lewis wants nothing to do with him

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Driscolls pray for justice as Megan's verdict is delivered. Abi identifies her evasive landlord, Sam makes peace with Nick, and Hope and Ruby plan a surprise for Tyrone

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire? Celebrity Special
programme.desc: A celebrity version of the big-prize quiz in which stars answer questions to win cash for their chosen charities. With lifelines including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Bradley & Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son hit the road together on a series of adventures

programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Argentina v C. Verde
programme.desc: Argentina v Cape Verde (Kick-off 11.00pm). Coverage of the round-of-32 match at Miami Stadium in the USA. Reigning champions Argentina topped Group J with a game to spare after talisman Lionel Messi scored all five of his team's goals in wins over Algeria and Austria. The progress of Cape Verde is arguably the story of the tournament so far, with the unfancied and unheralded minnows reaching this stage at the first attempt, courtesy of draws against Spain, Uruguay and Saudi Arabia

programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Colombia v Ghana
programme.desc: Colombia v Ghana (Kick-off 2.30am). Coverage of the final round-of-32 match, held at Kansas City Stadium in the USA. Colombia won Group K with seven points, courtesy of wins against DR Congo and Uzbekistan, and a goalless draw with Portugal. Ghana impressed at times in the group stage, holding England to a goalless draw and taking the lead against Croatia, before succumbing to a 2-1 defeat that left them third in Group L

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programme.title: Fletchers' Family Farm
programme.desc: There is an emergency on the farm when a ewe becomes sick, Kelvin readies the older piglets for market and a visit to A&E is in order after a run-in with a wasp's nest. A kitten becomes the newest member of the household and it is party time as Kelvin pulls out all the stops for a surprise birthday bash for Liz's 40th

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The next leg of his trip sees James heading to Bresse, home of one of the country's most famous ingredients - the Bresse chicken. He visits a chicken farm to discover just why these birds are so special, and gets the chance to cook his own. He also meets Georges Blanc, one of his culinary heroes, before attending the annual Michelin star football tournament

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: The chef meets a family who live off grid, samples fabada stew, Urbies cheese, and visits a restaurant famous for its sardines

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: New Zealand v France (Kick-off 8.10am). Ugo Monye presents all the action from the opening round-robin match of the inaugural rugby union competition, held at One New Zealand Stadium. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi and Sean Fitzpatrick, and commentary by Gareth Rhys Owen and Benjamin Kayser

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: Australia v Ireland (Kick-off 11.10am). Ugo Monye presents coverage of the rugby union match at Allianz Stadium in Sydney. Wallabies coach Joe Schmidt will have fond memories of his time in Ireland - he enjoyed a successful six year spell as head coach. His counterpart Andy Farrell has just signed a long term deal to lead the Irish team and will be hoping to build on a largely positive Six Nations campaign which saw them finish second to France. With analysis from Rory Best and George Gregan, and commentary by Martin Gillingham and Gordon D'Arcy

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: Fiji v Wales (Kick-off 2.10pm). Lauren Salter presents all the action from the rugby union match at Cardiff City Stadium. While Fiji are the designated hosts, the match is being played here because their main stadium does not meet the competition requirements, and Wales will be looking to take advantage of that and build on their long-awaited Six Nations victory over Italy in March. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Jamie Roberts and Dan Biggar, and commentary by Miles Harrison, Shane Williams and Ben Kay

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: ITV News and Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Canada v Morocco
programme.desc: Canada v Morocco (Kick-off 6.00pm). Coverage of the first round-of-16 match, which takes place at Houston Stadium in the USA. Canada edged past South Africa 1-0 in the last round, in a match that is unlikely to live long in the memory, but the Canadians won't let that bother them, although they'll be underdogs this time against a side looking to emulate their achievements of four years ago, when they reached the semi-finals. Morocco come into this match on the back of a penalty shootout victory over the Netherlands, secured thanks to a late equaliser from Fulham defender Issa Diop

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: Comedian Tom Allen celebrates the chaos that occurs when making TV shows with unseen outtakes from dramas, comedies and entertainment shows. This episode features an accidental innuendo with Idris Elba that leaves Lorraine in tears of laughter, Love Island filming that is interrupted by angry animals, and an interview with Hollywood star Paul Rudd that ends in disaster

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: ITV News and Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Dirty Dancing
programme.desc: A dance instructor at a 1960s resort hotel fears his career will be jeopardised when his regular partner is forced to pull out of an important show in which they are due to perform. Luckily, a naive teenager is happy to step in, and is treated to a whirlwind holiday education in life, love and expressing herself. Romantic drama, with Patrick Swayze, Jennifer Grey and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: The chef discovers how migas is made in Extremadura, meets an award-winning tortilla chef, and visits a local brewery. He also creates a garlic and bean stew, poussin, and a cherry beer and chocolate cake

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programme.title: Ainsley's Fantastic Flavours
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott delves into the tastes and flavours of North America, making his own version of a famous fast food chain's double burger, as well as peanut butter and jelly brownies. He is also joined by Ruby Bhogal, who tries to find out the secret behind the best fried chicken, and actress Sheree Murphy, who makes chicken pot pie

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to Normandy and sets up his mobile kitchen in one of the many calvados orchards in the area, making a dish of artichokes with hollandaise. He also visits restaurant Le Pavé d'Auge in Beuvron-en-Auge to meets a chef who likes to cook with as much butter as he does

programme.title: Thunderball
programme.desc: James Bond travels to the Bahamas on the trail of two nuclear warheads stolen from a British aircraft. The lethal weapons have fallen into the hands of international crime syndicate Spectre, which plans to fire them at the US coast unless an exorbitant ransom is paid. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Claudine Auger, Adolfo Celi and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan is joined by star of Gavin & Stacey Joanna Page, sports presenter and host of The High Performance Podcast Jake Humphrey, and India Hicks. Farmer Tom Davis brings along a pair of piglets to hog the limelight, and drinks expert Andy Clarke shares his favourite tipples inspired by the movies in the Best of British feature

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes Ellie Simmonds back for a second visit to her kitchen after the two got on so well the first time, with the five-time Paralympic swimming gold medallist baking a coffee and walnut cake. Prue's food hero is a tea producer from the Vale of Glamorgan who makes loose leaf tea and kombucha tea, while he has a versatile baked feta, roasted tomatoes and garlic dish on the menu and is pleased when a hack for egg yolks goes well

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team of gardening experts head to Waltham Abbey in Essex to create a tropical escape with a yoga deck and home office at its heart for Venessa Taylor, a selfless leukaemia survivor who campaigns to raise the awareness of blood cancer and the importance of bone marrow donations across all ethnic groups

programme.title: ITV News and Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a member of a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly

programme.title: You Only Live Twice
programme.desc: James Bond is sent on a mission to uncover the truth behind the disappearances of Soviet and American space capsules before the mutually suspicious superpowers go to war. The trail leads to Japan, where Bond finally comes face to face with his arch-enemy Blofeld. Spy thriller, starring Sean Connery, Donald Pleasence, Akiko Wakabayashi and Bernard Lee

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Nathan from Hitchin, Olivia originally from Guernsey, Michaela from Lincolnshire and Martyn from London pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Brazil v Norway
programme.desc: Brazil v Norway (Kick-off 9.00pm). Coverage of the third round-of-16 match, which takes place at New York/New Jersey Stadium in the USA. Brazil came from behind to beat Japan 2-1 in injury-time, having trailed at the break, with goals by Casemiro and Gabriel Martinelli breaking Japanese hearts. Despite not quite reaching their peak yet, Brazil will start as favourites here as they look to move a step closer towards a first World Cup triumph since 2002. Norway had Erling Braut Haaland to thank for a late winner in their last-32 match against Ivory Coast, after Amad Diallo's stunning goal had seemed enough to take the game into extra time

programme.title: TikTok: Murder Gone Viral
programme.desc: A look at the case of 17-year-old Charlie Cosser, who was fatally stabbed by 16-year-old Yura Varybrus at an end-of-term summer party for teenagers. Charlie's dad Martin made his dying son a promise that he would campaign for justice and awareness of knife crime, and his TikTok videos tell the story of a family struggling to come to terms with an act of senseless violence that tore their lives apart

programme.title: Ainsley's Taste of the Bahamas
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott explores the vibrant flavours, local producers, markets and unique dishes of the island nation located in the Atlantic Ocean. He kicks off his island adventures with the comfort foods at the heart of Bahamian cooking, from 'chicken in da bag' at Bamboo Shack to learning to put his peas before his rice with chef Tevin Kemp. Then he wraps things up in his beachside kitchen with prawn and coconut fritters served with a pickled coconut slaw

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts as contestant Ryan tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Coverage of the seventh round-of-16 match (Kick-off 5.00pm), which takes place at Atlanta Stadium in the USA. The fans in attendance will hope for a more entertaining affair than in the corresponding clash in Al Rayyan four years ago, as Morocco and Spain played out a monotonous 0-0 draw over 120 minutes, in which the Spanish dominated possession but did little with it as few chances were created. The tie came to life in the subsequent penalty shootout as the 2010 champions failed to convert any of their three attempts with goalkeeper Yassine Bounou saving from Sergio Busquets and Carlos Soler after Pablo Sarabia first hit the post, while the Atlas Lions scored three of their four penalties to progress. By missing their three spot kicks, Spain became only the second team to not score in a World Cup penalty shoot-out, following in the footsteps of Switzerland who lost to Ukraine in 2006 by the same 3-0 scoreline

programme.title: ITV Evening News; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Switzerland v Colombia or Ghana (Kick-off 9.00pm). Coverage of the eighth round-of-16 match, which takes place at BC Place Vancouver in Canada. Switzerland had a game to forget at the corresponding stage of the 2022 World Cup when they were thrashed 6-1 by Portugal. However, they will be hopeful of a much stronger showing this time around, having topped a group containing Canada, Bosnia and Herzegovina and Qatar, before easing past Algeria 2-0 in the round of 32

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Stephen Fry faces a grilling from a cast of autistic and learning-disabled interviewers. Did he take drugs in Buckingham Palace? Is he happy to be alive? As the questions come thick and fast, Stephen opens up about mental health, shame and self-criticism. There are probing questions about love, identity and belief, alongside moments of pure mischief and laughter - including an explosive question about his sexual preferences

programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Argentina v C. Verde
programme.desc: Argentina v Cape Verde (Kick-off 11.00pm). Coverage of the round-of-32 match at Miami Stadium in the USA. Reigning champions Argentina topped Group J with a game to spare after talisman Lionel Messi scored all five of his team's goals in wins over Algeria and Austria. The progress of Cape Verde is arguably the story of the tournament so far, with the unfancied and unheralded minnows reaching this stage at the first attempt, courtesy of draws against Spain, Uruguay and Saudi Arabia

programme.title: FIFA WC2026: Colombia v Ghana
programme.desc: Colombia v Ghana (Kick-off 2.30am). Coverage of the final round-of-32 match, held at Kansas City Stadium in the USA. Colombia won Group K with seven points, courtesy of wins against DR Congo and Uzbekistan, and a goalless draw with Portugal. Ghana impressed at times in the group stage, holding England to a goalless draw and taking the lead against Croatia, before succumbing to a 2-1 defeat that left them third in Group L

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy investigate the death of amateur artist Ruth Fairfax, whose body has been discovered by Joyce - but the more they delve into the dead woman's background, the more mysterious she becomes. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, with guest appearances by John Sessions and Leslie Phillips

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An organic bakery in the newly restored Larkton's Mill causes resentment in Lower Blissingham, with many of the old village businesses struggling, traffic having increased and rumours that owners Tom and Chrissie Larkton are planning to expand. Highwayman Inn landlady Denise Bantrig organises a protest group against the couple, and is supported by Maria, who runs the old village cafe and will not let her business go down without a fight. Their complaints are not taken too seriously until several people are dosed with a dangerous hallucinogenic at a Midsummer's Eve party, with Barnaby and Winter called in to investigate, and the situation escalates when a farmer is murdered. Crime drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Nick Hendrix

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Respected surgeon Dan Marsden is shot dead days before his retirement and his teenage daughters Mira and Karen are abducted. Joined by a new addition to the ranks, Vera investigates a mysterious parcel and a connection to the Iraq War as she races against time to catch the killer and save the girls. However, the case soon takes a violent and unexpected turn, striking at the heart of the team. Guest starring Saskia Reeves (Luther) and Dean Andrews (Ashes to Ashes), with Brenda Blethyn, David Leon and Gareth Farr

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' bed collapses and George finds himself short of cash to acquire a new one. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspected kidnapping and gets his first lead when a ransom note is discovered. The missing woman then turns up dead at the wheel of a crashed car, smelling of alcohol. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A book dealer is found drowned, having seemingly committed suicide in despair at having sunk all his cash in a local inventor's revolutionary new transport system - but the discovery of a second body makes it clear that there is a murderer at large. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of the village of Elverton-Cum-Latterly gather for its annual dominoes tournament, but this year it has competition from a drag queen event to benefit charity. This creates friction and soon one of the dominoes players is found dead and it is up to Barnaby to find the culprit. Mystery drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Nick Hendrix

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective delves into the past of extreme sports enthusiast Gideon Frane after he plunges from a cliff in a ball of flames. A scan of the files reveals a long list of stalking complaints against an ex, and an anonymous letter to the victim spelling out Burn in Hell is linked to one of her friends. Aided by DC Shepherd and the newly appointed DC Edwards, the team tries to track down Gideon's old gang to uncover a lifetime of bullying and an unexpected story of rough justice

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Trigger Point
programme.desc: Explosives officer Lana Washington and her team are called to investigate a suspected bomb in a black cab that has been abandoned on waste ground, but they soon realise they're at the site of something far more sinister. Working alongside their colleagues in counter-terrorism, the team races to find out what the person behind this unique bombing campaign wants, who they are and who they might be targeting next. Thriller, starring Vicky McClure

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor Mildred's hopes are raised when work-shy George looks for a job, but his chances of finding gainful employment seem slim. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.desc: Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: After resigning from his job as a traffic warden, henpecked George wonders how he can break the news to wife Mildred without incurring her wrath. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers babysit for their neighbours, but George misses his usual routine - so he nips out to a darts match with the infant in tow. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jasmine realises Shane is deeply involved in the MacFarlanes' affairs and searches his flat for incriminating evidence. Donna worries that Marlon will find out about her affair.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne leaves Liam Connor's funeral early to comfort Carla, while police break news to Kevin and Sally regarding Rosie Webster, and Tony desperately tries to comfort Carla.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony Gordon confesses to Carla Connor that he smashed the office window in a fit of rage, while Peter Barlow unexpectedly returns to Weatherfield alongside his young son Simon.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bernie reveals a hidden talent, while Phil Bellamy is babysitting Maggie Bolton's son Sam, but when he wakes up from a little nap to feed the baby, Sam has gone missing. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire, starring Kazia Pelka and Peter Benson

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A criminal's wife commits suicide, making Mike wish he had not been quite so forceful in extracting a confession. Meanwhile, Blaketon has the misfortune of getting mixed up in Greengrass's latest scheme to make a few bob. Rural drama, with Bill Maynard, Derek Fowlds and Michael Angelis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jasmine and Debbie are forced to act quickly when Ross arrives at Shane's flat. Paddy finds out about Donna's affair, while the Dingles put their plan to rescue Aaron into action.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Debbie persuades Eli to help dispose of the body and the conspirators get to work removing all evidence of the crime. Chas finds out more about Aaron's troubled past.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Deirdre realise Peter is not handling fatherhood well. DC Hooch arrives at Roy's Rolls and arrests Becky, while Tony feels angry as Carla leaves for Los Angeles.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rosie makes an ill-fated escape attempt, and Emily asks Norris if he is a transsexual, while Maria switches on Liam's mobile phone and finds all the calls he made to Carla.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress's determination to protect a widow's home from a gang of louts becomes an all-consuming obsession, while Greengrass gets his fingers burnt attempting to break into the fish and chip industry, little realising what a competitive business it is. Rural drama, starring William Simons and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass befriends a travelling musician (guest star Gary Barlow) who spreads a little disharmony in Aidensfield, while Jackie's parents drop in unexpectedly, casting a shadow over her relationship with Mike - and all at once domestic bliss becomes a thing of the past in the Bradley household. With Bill Maynard, Jason Durr and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam is suspended from duty and decides to spend some time with his son, while Brooks and Leyton make a breakthrough when they discover who was responsible for the train crash and put the culprit under surveillance. Jake and Kate argue over whether it was right to prosecute Finn Tyler in his fragile mental state. Drama, starring Paul Nicholls and Bradley Walsh

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a hotel room and the cash he was carrying has gone missing. Ronnie and Sam discover the victim owed his ex-wife thousands of pounds in child support for their terminally ill son and had been lying to his girlfriend about his line of work. As the investigation progresses, the detectives focus their inquiries on the child's grandfather. Did he commit murder to recoup the unpaid money or is there more to this family set-up than first appears? Meanwhile, at the Crown Prosecution Service, Kate and Jake work out their differences. Guest starring Ian Bleasdale (Casualty)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. A massive police search leads to the body of a missing five-year-old, the victim of sexual assault and strangulation. All the evidence points to a local drunkard being the guilty party _ but as the case goes to trial it becomes clear that the outcome is by no means assured. Gripping crime drama by Lynda La Plante, starring David Hayman, Kate Buffery, Helen McCrory and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The couple offer their services to a woman in labour, despite their lack of experience - though George does know how to dismantle a motorbike. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy tells Ross he will not let him ruin Donna's marriage. Jasmine, Eli and Debbie have a close call and Aaron tells a dismayed Chas he wants to live with his father.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eli is furious that Jasmine is saying too much when she tells Ashley that she has split up with Shane. Donna is upset when Ross decides to leave the village.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz tries to help John, but places herself in danger. Maria continues to fret over all the calls to Carla on Liam's phone, and Pam urges Molly to attend a wedding fayre.

programme.desc: John tells Fiz he kidnapped Rosie, and Maria decides to find out more about Carla's involvement in Lad Rags, while Steve urges Becky to start flirting with other men.

programme.desc: Mike and Jackie suffer a terrifying ordeal at the hands of a stalker out for revenge, and David holds the fort while Greengrass and Bernie help to arrange a funeral with a difference. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr, Fiona Dolman and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the death of a pensioner found stabbed on a train at the height of rush hour. The case has a huge impact on Joe's home life when his daughter is named as a key witness, and he struggles to maintain a professional distance. The trail leads the team to a quiet Northumberland coastal town where clues appear to lead to one particular street - but why are the residents so reluctant to talk? Starring Brenda Blethyn, with David Leon, Sonya Cassidy, Jon Morrison and Eva Birthistle

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam lead the investigation into the death of a wealthy woman whose body was dumped in the Thames. The team initially focuses inquiries on the victim's abusive ex-husband, with whom she had been involved in a bitter struggle over custody of their daughter Holly, but the case heads in a different direction when the detectives discover the teenager was infatuated with her mother's new partner. With Patrick Baladi and an appearance by Bradley Walsh's son Barney

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. But the accused points the finger back at Connie, claiming she had been deliberately starving her grandmother. Who will the team believe? With Jan Francis, Daniel Casey and Ramon Tikaram

programme.desc: Mrs Roper is astonished and thrilled when George buys her an expensive present for their anniversary. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.desc: Mrs Roper goes into hospital with suspected appendicitis and George looks forward to some peace and quiet - but Jerry shows up on his doorstep with other ideas. Roy Kinnear guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Angus, a member of the Bleakridge Watch, a group of villagers who walk the streets reporting anyone who falls foul of the law, is found dead in his meat freezer. Barnaby and Winter meet Angus's devastated sister and her daughter and learn that Angus was investigating a recent spate of burglaries in the village, but had yet to identify the culprit. Angus' post-mortem reveals strange restraining injuries on his wrists and the plot thickens as Barnaby discovers just how far some people will go for power. Frances Barber guest stars as the leader of the Watch

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Matthew struggles to contain his guilt as Anna gives a speech at Donald's funeral. Jasmine resolves to take control of her life and Gennie is struck by stage fright.

programme.desc: David and Pollard look in on the choir's latest rehearsals, while Gennie finds an unusual way to stay out of the limelight. Rodney worries that Lily's condition is worsening.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rosie escapes from John's clutches, and Maria is tormented by a suspicion that something was going on between Liam and Carla, while Ken is forced to buy fireworks for Simon.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rosie defends John's actions, and Janice prepares for her court appearance, while Tony learns he has amassed huge debts and needs to start selling the apartments in a hurry.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local church suffers a series of thefts and the finger of suspicion seems to point to the vicarage, of all places. Meanwhile, Greengrass's hopes of winning a dog show take a knock when his pedigree contestant goes missing. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A former MP's lascivious past catches up with him just as he is planning a comfortable retirement, and Greengrass makes his presence felt at the village bonfire party as only he knows how. Rural drama, starring Bill Maynard, William Russell and Joanna David

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Following the brutal murder of a gay man, Sam and Ronnie embark on a desperate mission to track down the victim's adoptive son, who has gone missing. The prime suspect soon emerges as the child's biological father - an alleged violent homophobe unable to stomach the idea of the youngster being brought up in a same-sex household - but was he acting alone? Guest starring Glynis Barber, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Return of the detective drama starring Bradley Walsh. Ronnie and new partner Joe lead the investigation into the death of jeweller Harry Bernstein, who has been found with his hands and teeth missing. His wife, her lover and a former business associate are all possible suspects, but the case takes a surprising turn when the victim's sister receives a gruesome delivery. She's on the jury for the trial of a drug dealer and murderer who Ronnie has been trying to nail for years, and the threat to her points to jury tampering, so Jake and Kate have to take a dramatic approach to win the case. The problem is, they're up against a formidable defence barrister. Co-starring Ben Bailey Smith, Dominic Rowan and Georgia Taylor, with guests Helen Baxendale and Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Lynda La Plante's crime drama. DI Pat North investigates the case of a woman abducted in broad daylight, but a sudden murder confession leaves the squad with more questions than answers. Meanwhile, DCS Michael Walker begins to fear for his children's safety at the hands of his ex-wife's new boyfriend. Kate Buffery and David Hayman star

programme.desc: Winter and Barnaby investigate when Lower Pampling's star cricketer Leo Henderson is found dead after a match in the grounds, pummelled to death by cricket balls. Barnaby is surprised to see DS Jones undercover as one of Leo's team-mates, and his former colleague reveals he is working on suspected cases of match-fixing in the area, but things take an even more sinister turn when captain Fitz Theara is stabbed through the heart with a cricket stump

programme.desc: The choir members are full of confidence as they prepare for the contest, but David and Pollard plan to sabotage their efforts. Rodney begs Lily to tell Edna about her condition.

programme.desc: Fiz manages to convince the police she had no part in John's plan, and Sally watches Rosie's footage of Carla and Liam, while Peter decides to abandon Simon.

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mildred faces the prospect of becoming a step-grandmother when George's past catches up with him. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chas makes an effort to bond with her son after the youngster is arrested for possession of drugs. Donna asks Ross to be patient as she tries to end her relationship with Marlon.

programme.desc: Jasmine tells Shane she plans to keep investigating the MacFarlanes. Chas enlists Donna's help in looking for her son Aaron - leaving Marlon worried about his wife's safety.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rosie is dragged back to the room by her frantic captor, who plants her purse in a dingy pub for someone to find. An emotionally overwrought Carla tells Tony the wedding is off.

programme.desc: Carla breaks down at Liam's funeral and has to leave the church, while Sally is forced to miss the service altogether because the police have new information about Rosie.

programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.desc: A handsome new locum wins Maggie's heart but soon falls out of favour with the locals when a misdiagnosis results in tragedy. Rural drama, starring Kazia Pelka and Bill Maynard

Meanwhile, former copper Blaketon's well-honed instincts alert him to council corruption. Nostalgic rural drama, starring Bill Maynard, Derek Fowlds and Jason Durr

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony Gordon confesses to Carla Connor that he smashed the office window in a fit of rage, while Peter Barlow unexpectedly returns to Weatherfield alongside his young son Simon.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bernie reveals a hidden talent, while Phil Bellamy is babysitting Maggie Bolton's son Sam, but when he wakes up from a little nap to feed the baby, Sam has gone missing. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire, starring Kazia Pelka and Peter Benson

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A criminal's wife commits suicide, making Mike wish he had not been quite so forceful in extracting a confession. Meanwhile, Blaketon has the misfortune of getting mixed up in Greengrass's latest scheme to make a few bob. Rural drama, with Bill Maynard, Derek Fowlds and Michael Angelis

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. The second victim survives and is able to identify her assailant, but she and Casey fall for each other - a relationship that threatens to damage both the CPS's case and the detective's career. Guest starring Lydia Leonard, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Georgia Taylor (Coronation Street, Casualty) and Paterson Joseph (Peep Show) join the cast as the crime drama returns for its seventh series. In the first of a two-part story, a suicidal man causes a train crash when he drives onto a railway crossing, killing 15 people. With Jake prosecuting, the man's defence barrister tries to prove his vulnerability and mental illness, but then a twist in the investigation leaves Sam's career hanging in the balance. Starring Bradley Walsh, Dominic Rowan and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Part two of two. The past catches up with Frost as he investigates a series of murders that appear to mirror crimes he failed to solve more than 16 years ago. As the case progresses, Mullett becomes convinced the detective's life is in danger, and with Jack now trying to bring two killers to justice - the copycat and the assailant at the dogfight - tragedy cannot be far ahead. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Phyllis Logan, Niamh Cusack, Adrian Dunbar, Leah Bracknell and Simon Shepherd

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers suffer a domestic crisis when Mildred discovers George has been writing to another woman. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce
