programme.desc: Matt Chapman and the team are at Sandown Park ahead of today's racing

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: Japan v Italy (Kick-off 9.40am). All the action from the round-robin match at Tokyo Chichibunomiya Stadium. Japan will have to make do without their coach Eddie Jones, as he is is currently serving a four-match suspension after verbally abusing a match official at an under-23 tournament. Italy will be looking to build on an impressive Six Nations campaign, which saw them chalk up a first-ever win against England. With Commentary from Claire Thomas and Jim Hamilton

World champions South Africa's side is packed with talent including Pieter-Steph du Toit, Eben Etzebeth and new fly-half Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Danny Care and Bryan Habana

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: Argentina v Scotland (Kick-off 8.10pm). All the action from the rugby union match at Estadio Mario Alberto Kempes in Cordoba. Argentina have won three of the five meetings between the teams this decade, including the most recent contest, which saw them prevail 33-24 at Murrayfield last November. Scotland were 14-0 up at half-time on that occasion and extended that lead to 21-0 in the second half, but the Pumas fought back to claim a famous comeback victory

programme.desc: Action from November 1979, featuring Crystal Palace v Manchester City, Derby County v West Bromwich Albion and Sunderland v Chelsea

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara present the second part of the semi-final, with the best competitors from four heats testing their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing monk defends a man accused of murdering a wealthy and powerful baron, with only a mysterious leper as his ally. Medieval drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver and Sarah Badel

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of an aristocrat's butler, whose erudition seems strangely at odds with his lowly occupation, presents Holmes with a fresh mystery to solve. The key to the case lies in an ancient family ritual - and points the way to a historic treasure. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: The Napoleonic War is entering its final stages and the French formulate a desperate plan to capture Wellington's spymaster Major General Ross. Sharpe is ordered by Wellington to accompany Colonel Brand, whose loyalty is questioned by both Wellington and Ross, as he intends to destroy the French main powder supply in the caves of Rocha. Meanwhile, Sharpe's wife is showing signs of being bored with military life and enjoys the attentions of a young poet. Sean Bean stars, with Daragh O'Malley, Mark Strong and Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Framed by long-time enemy Ducos for stealing Napoleon's treasures, Sharpe is abandoned by his wife Jane, and goes on the run from both the English and the French until he can prove his innocence. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: The Debrief with Dan and ROG
programme.desc: Claire Thomas is joined by Dan Biggar and Ronan O'Gara to analyse the best of the action from the opening weekend of the inaugural Nations Championship

programme.title: Hot Fuzz
programme.desc: An overly zealous, by-the-book London policeman is reassigned to a sleepy country village where the crime rate is virtually zero, and teamed up with an eager but dimwitted partner. But just as the officer's uneventful new environment begins to chafe, a grisly series of so-called accidents gives him reason to suspect a strange conspiracy is afoot. Action comedy, with Simon Pegg, Nick Frost, Jim Broadbent, Timothy Dalton and Paddy Considine

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry agrees to protect an ex-convict who is being targeted by former partners wanting a share of money stolen during a bank heist. Meanwhile, Arthur resorts to devious tactics to obtain the location of the stashed loot first. Dennis Waterman and George Cole star

programme.title: Formula E Highlights
programme.desc: The Shanghai E-Prix. Action from the 12th and 13th races of the campaign, which came from Shanghai International Circuit in China. Mitch Evans went into this meeting on top of the standings, with Oliver Rowland and Edoardo Mortara second and third respectively, but all three failed to finish last time out in Sanya

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Part two of two. Motorcycling enthusiast Henry Cole gets an experience of the Wall of Death and meets the wives and girlfriends of leading British Superbike Championship contenders. He also learns about the work of a charity that aims to get riders back on two wheels after serious accidents, and tries his hand at moto gymkhana. Plus, a look at how the presenter's Streetfighter bike fared at the Brighton Custom Show

programme.title: The World's End
programme.desc: A 40-year-old man who refuses to grow up drags four reluctant childhood friends back to their home town to recreate a pub crawl they attempted in their teens. As they work their way round the 12 local bars, they stumble on a threat to the entire human race. Sci-fi comedy, starring Simon Pegg, Nick Frost and Martin Freeman

programme.desc: Danielle is set a stiff challenge, and Mike is desperate to buy a 1967 Ford Fairlane car

programme.title: ITV Racing: Live from Sandown
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of a meeting headlined by the Coral-Eclipse from Sandown Park, plus racing from Newmarket. With analysis from Jason Weaver, commentary by Richard Hoiles and reports from Matt Chapman and Sally Ann Grassick

programme.title: The Best of the 90s
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of the wide variety of sports covered by ITV during the 1990s, including the 1990 World Cup and Euro 96

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: South Africa v England (Kick-off 4.40pm). Coverage of the rugby union match at Ellis Park in Johannesburg. England have lost their last four games, but will take solace from their much-improved performance against France in March. World champions South Africa's side is packed with talent including Pieter-Steph du Toit, Eben Etzebeth and new fly-half Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Danny Care and Bryan Habana

programme.title: The Best of the 80s
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV during the 1980s, ranging from football and boxing to wrestling and double decker racing

programme.title: Nations Championship
programme.desc: Argentina v Scotland (Kick-off 8.10pm). All the action from the rugby union match at Estadio Mario Alberto Kempes in Cordoba. Argentina have won three of the five meetings between the teams this decade, including the most recent contest, which saw them prevail 33-24 at Murrayfield last November. Scotland were 14-0 up at half-time on that occasion and extended that lead to 21-0 in the second half, but the Pumas fought back to claim a famous comeback victory

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling - the roster features world-class talent including Jon Moxley and Claudio Castagnoli, and many more who bring a freshness and energy into professional wrestling

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The Daleys move into the high-spending world of corporate hospitality and find themselves caught between glamour and danger. Samantha Womack guest stars, alongside George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is unhappy to learn that Kandi has been living with Alan in his house for the past three weeks - especially since he himself has been going through a spell of bad luck with women. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan tries to win Kandi back, and Charlie takes a shine to her mother. Meanwhile, Judith is set up with Kandi's father Andy. Guest starring Kevin Sorbo, Gail O'Grady and April Bowlby

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Action from November 1979, featuring Crystal Palace v Manchester City, Derby County v West Bromwich Albion and Sunderland v Chelsea

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara present the second part of the semi-final, with the best competitors from four heats testing their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing monk defends a man accused of murdering a wealthy and powerful baron, with only a mysterious leper as his ally. Medieval drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver and Sarah Badel

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of an aristocrat's butler, whose erudition seems strangely at odds with his lowly occupation, presents Holmes with a fresh mystery to solve. The key to the case lies in an ancient family ritual - and points the way to a historic treasure. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: The Napoleonic War is entering its final stages and the French formulate a desperate plan to capture Wellington's spymaster Major General Ross. Sharpe is ordered by Wellington to accompany Colonel Brand, whose loyalty is questioned by both Wellington and Ross, as he intends to destroy the French main powder supply in the caves of Rocha. Meanwhile, Sharpe's wife is showing signs of being bored with military life and enjoys the attentions of a young poet. Sean Bean stars, with Daragh O'Malley, Mark Strong and Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Framed by long-time enemy Ducos for stealing Napoleon's treasures, Sharpe is abandoned by his wife Jane, and goes on the run from both the English and the French until he can prove his innocence. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: The Debrief with Dan and ROG
programme.desc: Claire Thomas is joined by Dan Biggar and Ronan O'Gara to analyse the best of the action from the opening weekend of the inaugural Nations Championship

programme.title: Hot Fuzz
programme.desc: An overly zealous, by-the-book London policeman is reassigned to a sleepy country village where the crime rate is virtually zero, and teamed up with an eager but dimwitted partner. But just as the officer's uneventful new environment begins to chafe, a grisly series of so-called accidents gives him reason to suspect a strange conspiracy is afoot. Action comedy, with Simon Pegg, Nick Frost, Jim Broadbent, Timothy Dalton and Paddy Considine

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: Robin loses a valuable painting and racks of expensive vintage wine in a poker game against the publisher of a men's magazine - and tries to save face by gambling his entire estate on the outcome of a softball match. Tom Selleck stars

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: TC visits Detroit, where he's forced to take part in a bare-knuckle boxing bout to repay a debt to an old friend. Tom Selleck stars, with Roger E Mosley and Shannen Doherty

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: In order to investigate a villain's idea of the perfect crime, Harriet poses as a prostitute and Jim goes up in the world. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: Ken is hired to watch an errant wife when an Argentinian polo team comes to Woodcote. Michael Elphick and David Daker star

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: Richard and Sharron take on bodyguard duties for a foreign president and his wife during a submarine trip - but the duo must keep their wits about them when an assassin finds their way on board. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt and Alexandra Bastedo

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry agrees to protect an ex-convict who is being targeted by former partners wanting a share of money stolen during a bank heist. Meanwhile, Arthur resorts to devious tactics to obtain the location of the stashed loot first. Dennis Waterman and George Cole star

programme.title: Formula E Highlights
programme.desc: The Shanghai E-Prix. Action from the 12th and 13th races of the campaign, which came from Shanghai International Circuit in China. Mitch Evans went into this meeting on top of the standings, with Oliver Rowland and Edoardo Mortara second and third respectively, but all three failed to finish last time out in Sanya

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Part two of two. Motorcycling enthusiast Henry Cole gets an experience of the Wall of Death and meets the wives and girlfriends of leading British Superbike Championship contenders. He also learns about the work of a charity that aims to get riders back on two wheels after serious accidents, and tries his hand at moto gymkhana. Plus, a look at how the presenter's Streetfighter bike fared at the Brighton Custom Show

programme.title: The World's End
programme.desc: A 40-year-old man who refuses to grow up drags four reluctant childhood friends back to their home town to recreate a pub crawl they attempted in their teens. As they work their way round the 12 local bars, they stumble on a threat to the entire human race. Sci-fi comedy, starring Simon Pegg, Nick Frost and Martin Freeman

programme.title: BattleBots

programme.desc: The second leg of the UK tour sees Ali Hamidi and Bobby Zamora attempt to catch a monster shark over 400lbs from just off the north Devon coast. They then venture to Norfolk in pursuit of Britain's biggest freshwater predator - the wels catfish. The duo's mission is to catch a giant over 50lbs. Chef Daniel Clifford also pays the lads another visit for a bank side cook-a-long.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers: Deadliest Roads
programme.desc: The three truckers are sent on solo missions on India's roads. Newbie Dave Redmon faces the Freefall Freeway for the first time, a treacherous journey for which he and his truck are blessed before setting off. Lisa Kelly heads back to the Ledge, the scene of her mini-breakdown - but this time a rockslide gives her the chance to redeem herself - and Rick Yemm's anger boils over once more, until the attention he receives from a group of local children helps him put things in perspective

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling - the roster features world-class talent including Jon Moxley and Claudio Castagnoli, and many more who bring a freshness and energy into professional wrestling

programme.title: Dark Side of the Ring
programme.desc: When wrestling in the mid-1990s is rocked by accusations of rampant steroid abuse, prosecutors set out to prove that promotion owner Vince McMahon is the one responsible

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is held hostage by former friend Frankie Connor, who is now a prisoner on the run and desperate to know whether his wife is having an affair with Italian restaurateur Luigi. Final episode of the light-hearted drama, starring George Cole, Gary Webster and Glynn Edwards

programme.title: The Best of the 70s
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV, including horse racing, Formula One and darts

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is nominated for an advertising award but decides to boycott the ceremony, having lost to a rival several years in a row. The rest of the family try to con him into going. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen

programme.desc: Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Coverage of the third T20 in the five-match series, which takes place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham. The last time these two sides met at this venue was in the third and final T20 of a series in 2022. India had already won the series after winning the first two games but England managed to claim a consolation victory. They made 215-7 in their 20 overs before restricting the visitors to 198-9 as they won by 17 runs

programme.desc: Surrey v Warwickshire Bears. Coverage of the League One match at Kia Oval in London. programme.title: Live Vitality T20 Blast Cricket
programme.desc: Surrey v Sussex Sharks. Coverage of the South Group match at Kia Oval in London. These two sides met at this ground last season in a group match that was won easily by the hosts. Surrey made 210-6 in their 20 overs with Dom Sibley leading the way with 45 from 32 balls, before Sussex were bowled out for 162 with a ball of their innings remaining. Mitchell Santner took 3-26 from his four overs to help Surrey to a 48-run victory

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Coverage of the second T20 in the five-match series from Emirates Old Trafford, Manchester. The visitors have dominated England in this format of the game over the last four years, winning eight of the last 11 meetings since July 2022. The last time these two teams met at this venue was in July 2018 when India won by eight wickets as they claimed victory in the first of three T20's in England that summer

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Coverage of the third T20 in the five-match series, which takes place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham. The last time these two sides met at this venue was in the third and final T20 of a series in 2022. India had already won the series after winning the first two games but England managed to claim a consolation victory. They made 215-7 in their 20 overs before restricting the visitors to 198-9 as they won by 17 runs

programme.title: Live Women's Vitality T20 Blast
programme.desc: Surrey v Warwickshire Bears. Coverage of the League One match at Kia Oval in London. Both these sides had a strong season last year and met in the final of this competition 12 months ago. The Bears made 153-9 in their 20 overs with Issy Wong top-scoring with 31 from 19 balls. Unfortunately for Warwickshire it wasn't enough as Surrey chased down their target with 20 balls remaining to win by five wickets

programme.title: Live Vitality T20 Blast Cricket
programme.desc: Surrey v Sussex Sharks. Coverage of the South Group match at Kia Oval in London. These two sides met at this ground last season in a group match that was won easily by the hosts. Surrey made 210-6 in their 20 overs with Dom Sibley leading the way with 45 from 32 balls, before Sussex were bowled out for 162 with a ball of their innings remaining. Mitchell Santner took 3-26 from his four overs to help Surrey to a 48-run victory

programme.desc: Notts Outlaws v Lancashire Lightning. Coverage of the North Group match from Trent Bridge, Nottingham. programme.title: Live Formula 1
programme.desc: The British F1 Grand Prix (Start-time 3.00pm). Coverage of the ninth round of the campaign, held at Silverstone. Home favourite Lando Norris took the chequered flag here last year for McLaren, with his teammate Oscar Piastri finishing second and Sauber's Nico Hülkenberg claiming his maiden podium in third. Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton will be looking to extend his record of most wins at this Grand Prix having already won nine races here between 2008 and 2024

programme.desc: Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur. Action from the 2009 final at Wembley Stadium, where the first major trophy of the season was contested. programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the 20212 final at Wembley Stadium. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Swansea City. Highlights of the 2013 final at Wembley Stadium, where the League Two Bantams were aiming to produce yet another cup upset. Swansea were appearing in their first major cup final and beat Chelsea in the semi-finals to reach this stage, but Bradford had stolen most of the headlines in the competition this season, belying their lowly league status to eliminate the likes of Arsenal, Aston Villa and Wigan Athletic

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the 20212 final at Wembley Stadium. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset

programme.desc: Bradford City v Swansea City. Highlights of the 2013 final at Wembley Stadium, where the League Two Bantams were aiming to produce yet another cup upset. programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Swansea City. Highlights of the 2013 final at Wembley Stadium, where the League Two Bantams were aiming to produce yet another cup upset. Swansea were appearing in their first major cup final and beat Chelsea in the semi-finals to reach this stage, but Bradford had stolen most of the headlines in the competition this season, belying their lowly league status to eliminate the likes of Arsenal, Aston Villa and Wigan Athletic

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Coverage of the third T20 in the five-match series, which takes place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham. The last time these two sides met at this venue was in the third and final T20 of a series in 2022. India had already won the series after winning the first two games but England managed to claim a consolation victory. They made 215-7 in their 20 overs before restricting the visitors to 198-9 as they won by 17 runs

programme.title: Live Women's Vitality T20 Blast
programme.desc: Surrey v Warwickshire Bears. Coverage of the League One match at Kia Oval in London. Both these sides had a strong season last year and met in the final of this competition 12 months ago. The Bears made 153-9 in their 20 overs with Issy Wong top-scoring with 31 from 19 balls. Unfortunately for Warwickshire it wasn't enough as Surrey chased down their target with 20 balls remaining to win by five wickets

programme.title: Live Vitality T20 Blast Cricket
programme.desc: Surrey v Sussex Sharks. Coverage of the South Group match at Kia Oval in London. These two sides met at this ground last season in a group match that was won easily by the hosts. Surrey made 210-6 in their 20 overs with Dom Sibley leading the way with 45 from 32 balls, before Sussex were bowled out for 162 with a ball of their innings remaining. Mitchell Santner took 3-26 from his four overs to help Surrey to a 48-run victory

programme.title: Live DP World Tour Golf
programme.desc: The Genesis Scottish Open. Coverage of day one of the Rolex Series event, held at Renaissance Club in North Berwick. Chris Gotterup of America was the only winner of a Rolex Series tournament from outside of Europe last year when he finished two strokes clear of Northern Ireland's Rory McIlroy and Marco Penge of England here to claim his maiden DP World Tour title

programme.title: Live LPGA Tour Golf
programme.desc: The Amundi Evian Championship. Coverage of day one of the Major at Evian Resort Golf Club in Évian-les-Bains, France. Australia's Grace Kim is the reigning champion after she defeated Thailand's Jeeno Thitikul in a play-off last year, after both players finished the tournament on an overall score of 14-under par

programme.title: Live Vitality T20 Blast Cricket
programme.desc: Notts Outlaws v Lancashire Lightning. Coverage of the North Group match from Trent Bridge, Nottingham. These two sides last met at this venue in July last year when the Outlaws claimed victory thanks to a four-wicket win in a low-scoring affair. programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Coverage of the third T20 in the five-match series, which takes place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham. The last time these two sides met at this venue was in the third and final T20 of a series in 2022. India had already won the series after winning the first two games but England managed to claim a consolation victory. They made 215-7 in their 20 overs before restricting the visitors to 198-9 as they won by 17 runs

programme.title: Live Women's Vitality T20 Blast
programme.desc: Surrey v Warwickshire Bears. Coverage of the League One match at Kia Oval in London. Both these sides had a strong season last year and met in the final of this competition 12 months ago. The Bears made 153-9 in their 20 overs with Issy Wong top-scoring with 31 from 19 balls. Unfortunately for Warwickshire it wasn't enough as Surrey chased down their target with 20 balls remaining to win by five wickets

programme.title: Live Vitality T20 Blast Cricket
programme.desc: Surrey v Sussex Sharks. Coverage of the South Group match at Kia Oval in London. These two sides met at this ground last season in a group match that was won easily by the hosts. Surrey made 210-6 in their 20 overs with Dom Sibley leading the way with 45 from 32 balls, before Sussex were bowled out for 162 with a ball of their innings remaining. Mitchell Santner took 3-26 from his four overs to help Surrey to a 48-run victory

programme.title: Live: Tour de France Men
programme.desc: Coverage from the first stage of this year's race, a 19.6km team time trial around Barcelona. This is the first of three stages that start in Spain before moving into France, as the 113th edition of the famous race gets underway. Last year's race was won by Tadej Pogacar who picked his fourth Tour de France title after previously winning it in 2020, 2021 and 2024. The cyclist from Slovenia won four of the 21 stages 12 months ago to join Great Britain's Chris Froome in winning the title four times

programme.desc: Coverage from the second stage of this year's race, a 168.5km route starting in Tarragona and ending in Barcelona. This stage last year was won by Dutch cyclist Mathieu van der Poel after he came through a 209.1km hill stage that ran from Lauwin-Planque to Boulogne-sur-Mer. He became the first of two Dutch participants to win a stage last year as Thymen Arensman picked up two wins later on in the race

programme.title: Live: Tour de France Men
programme.desc: Coverage from the first stage of this year's race, a 19.6km team time trial around Barcelona. This is the first of three stages that start in Spain before moving into France, as the 113th edition of the famous race gets underway. Last year's race was won by Tadej Pogacar who picked his fourth Tour de France title after previously winning it in 2020, 2021 and 2024. The cyclist from Slovenia won four of the 21 stages 12 months ago to join Great Britain's Chris Froome in winning the title four times

Sri Lanka will have to wait a lot longer to play their next match in this format, as they don't play a Test match until they face New Zealand away in early February

programme.desc: Chicago Cubs v St. Louis Cardinals (Start-time 7.30pm). Coverage of the National League match from Wrigley Field, as the sides play the third and final game of the series. The two teams last met in St. Louis in another three-match contest that saw the Cardinals win the series 2-1. The visitors won the first game 6-5 before the Cubs levelled the series with a 6-1 victory. The final match saw the Cardinals win 5-1 to pick up the series win

programme.title: Live MLB
programme.desc: Chicago Cubs v St. Louis Cardinals (Start-time 7.30pm). Coverage of the National League match from Wrigley Field, as the sides play the third and final game of the series. The two teams last met in St. Louis in another three-match contest that saw the Cardinals win the series 2-1. The visitors won the first game 6-5 before the Cubs levelled the series with a 6-1 victory. The final match saw the Cardinals win 5-1 to pick up the series win

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Having grown up just two hours from Belfast, Anna is excited to visit for the first time and meet boxing champion Carl Frampton, as he shows her around his home city. Anna learns about the local malt loaf staple veda bread and cooks for tourists at the birthplace of the Titanic. Carl puts Anna through her paces in the boxing ring and brings her to one of the city's last traditional bakeries, where she discovers the local sweet treat, fifteens

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Coverage of day six of the third Grand Slam tournament of the year, held at the All England Club, featuring men's and ladies' third-round singles matches. Eventual champion Jannik Sinner opened the day's proceedings on Centre Court at this stage last year, and he claimed a comfortable 6-1, 6-3, 6-1 victory over Pedro Martínez. Sinner went into this year's tournament highly fancied to retain the title, especially given Carlos Alcaraz's absence due to injury, but he struggled with cramp in the French Open and was eliminated by Juan Manuel Cerúndolo in the second round, ending a streak of nine consecutive Grand Slams that had been won by either him or Alcaraz

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents further coverage of day six of the third Grand Slam tournament of the year, held at the All England Club, featuring men's and ladies' third-round singles matches. Novak Djokovic concluded the sixth day's play on Centre Court last year with a dominant 6-3, 6-0, 6-4 victory over his Serbian compatriot Miomir Kecmanovic. There would be no eighth Wimbledon title for the modern era's most decorated man, as he was eliminated by Jannik Sinner at the semi-final stage, but few would have bet against him enjoying another deep run this time around

programme.desc: Paraguay v France (Kick-off 10.00pm). Coverage of the second round-of-16 match, which takes place at Philadelphia Stadium in the USA. The South Americans caused an upset in the last round, providing a defensive masterclass to edge past Germany in a dramatic penalty shootout, following a 1-1 draw. They'll need a similarly heroic display here against France, who are the tournament favourites, bang in form and awash with attacking talent

Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents further coverage of day seven of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, featuring men's and ladies' fourth-round singles matches. Home favourite Cameron Norrie eventually came through an attritional contest with Chilean qualifier Nicolás Jarry at this stage last year, prevailing 6-3, 7-6, 6-7, 6-7, 6-3. He was eventually followed onto the No. 1 Court by Amanda Anisimova of America and Czechia's Linda Nosková, with eventual runner-up Anisimova winning 6-2, 5-7, 6-4

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents continued coverage of day seven of the Grand Slam event from the All England Club, featuring fourth-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. This day was traditionally a rest day at SW19, but that was changed in 2022, with one of the main reasons being that advances in grass technology and maintenance meaning the courts no longer required a day of recovery mid-tournament. The crowd were treated to a strong performance by Carlos Alcaraz against a competitive opponent on the seventh day last year, with the 2023 and 2024 champion defeating Andrey Rublev 6-7, 6-3, 6-4, 6-4. However, the Spaniard will not be present this year due to a wrist injury, leaving the likes of Rublev with possibly their best chance yet of winning a Grand Slam

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Mexico v England (Kick-off 1.00am). Coverage of the fourth round-of-16 match, which takes place at Mexico City Stadium. The co-hosts beat Ecuador 2-0 in the last round, thanks to first-half goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez, and have to concede in the tournament so far. Facing the Mexicans at this venue is a particularly tough test, as they have such an excellent record here over the years, and England undoubtedly need to improve if they are to progress to the quarter-finals, having scraped past DR Congo 2-1 in the previous round thanks to two goals by Harry Kane

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Portugal v Spain (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the fifth round-of-16 match, which takes place at Dallas Stadium in the USA. Portugal finished second in their group, below Colombia and above DR Congo and Uzbekistan with two draws and a win before coming back from behind in their round-of-32 match with Croatia to prevail 2-1. Spain got off to a disappointing start with a goalless draw against Cape Verde, but they are still yet to concede in this tournament, having followed that result up with wins against Saudi Arabia and Uruguay in the group stage, before comfortably defeating Austria 3-0 in the next round. However, this is likely to be their toughest test so far, and the last meeting between the Iberian rivals saw Portugal win on penalties after a 2-2 draw in the final of the 2024/25 UEFA Nations League in Germany

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: USA v Belgium (Kick-off 1.00am). Coverage of the sixth round-of-16 match, which takes place at Seattle Stadium in the USA. The USA have made the most of home advantage and have made serene progress through to this stage, including a 2-0 win against Bosnia and Herzegovina in the last round. They will fancy their chances against a Belgian side who have looked a shadow of their former selves, but who pulled off a remarkable win against Senegal in the last 32, having been 2-0 down heading into the final minutes

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: Ashley John-Baptiste presents the case of two police officers murdered after being lured to a house in Manchester by a bogus 999 call. programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Switzerland v Algeria (Kick-off 4.00am). Coverage of the round-of-32 match at BC Place Vancouver in Canada. Switzerland finished top of Group B after they beat co-hosts Canada 2-1 in their final group game thanks to second-half goals from Ruben Vargas and Johan Manzambi. That victory left them on seven points while Canada had to settle for second place on four points, and they face an Algerian side who booked their place with a dramatic 3-3 draw against Austria in their final group match

Taylor Fritz and Alejandro Davidovich Fokina were first up on Centre Court on the fifth day last year, and it was fifth seed Fritz who took the honours in a 6-4, 6-3, 6-7, 6-1 victory. Carlos Alcaraz followed them, and the 2023 and 2024 champion triumphed 6-1, 3-6, 6-3, 6-4 against Jan-Lennard Struff. programme.desc: Australia v Egypt (Kick-off 7.00pm). Coverage of the round-of-32 match at Dallas Stadium in the USA. Australia finished second in Group D behind United States after they picked up four points from their three matches. They did lose 2-0 to the co-hosts but a 2-0 victory over Turkiye and a 0-0 draw against Paraguay meant they progressed to the knockout stages for the second successive World Cup. Egypt came through their group undefeated, beating New Zealand and drawing with Belgium and Iran, with the winners expected to face Argentina in the last 16

programme.desc: Coverage of day six of the third Grand Slam tournament of the year, held at the All England Club, featuring men's and ladies' third-round singles matches. Eventual champion Jannik Sinner opened the day's proceedings on Centre Court at this stage last year, and he claimed a comfortable 6-1, 6-3, 6-1 victory over Pedro Martínez. Sinner went into this year's tournament highly fancied to retain the title, especially given Carlos Alcaraz's absence due to injury, but he struggled with cramp in the French Open and was eliminated by Juan Manuel Cerúndolo in the second round, ending a streak of nine consecutive Grand Slams that had been won by either him or Alcaraz

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents continued coverage of day seven of the Grand Slam event from the All England Club, featuring fourth-round matches in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. This day was traditionally a rest day at SW19, but that was changed in 2022, with one of the main reasons being that advances in grass technology and maintenance meaning the courts no longer required a day of recovery mid-tournament. The crowd were treated to a strong performance by Carlos Alcaraz against a competitive opponent on the seventh day last year, with the 2023 and 2024 champion defeating Andrey Rublev 6-7, 6-3, 6-4, 6-4. However, the Spaniard will not be present this year due to a wrist injury, leaving the likes of Rublev with possibly their best chance yet of winning a Grand Slam

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Portugal v Spain (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the fifth round-of-16 match, which takes place at Dallas Stadium in the USA. Portugal finished second in their group, below Colombia and above DR Congo and Uzbekistan with two draws and a win before coming back from behind in their round-of-32 match with Croatia to prevail 2-1. Spain got off to a disappointing start with a goalless draw against Cape Verde, but they are still yet to concede in this tournament, having followed that result up with wins against Saudi Arabia and Uruguay in the group stage, before comfortably defeating Austria 3-0 in the next round. However, this is likely to be their toughest test so far, and the last meeting between the Iberian rivals saw Portugal win on penalties after a 2-2 draw in the final of the 2024/25 UEFA Nations League in Germany

programme.desc: Further coverage of day 10 of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, where the singles quarter-finals are scheduled to conclude. Novak Djokovic reached his 14th Wimbledon semi-final last year when he claimed a 6-7, 6-2, 7-5, 6-4 over Flavio Cobolli. However, Cobolli reached the final of the recent French Open, while Djokovic was surprisingly eliminated in the third round, so it remained to be seen who would progress further this time around

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents coverage of day 11 of the Grand Slam tournament from the All England Club, featuring the ladies' singles semi-finals. Centre Court was treated to a back-and-forth opening last-four match in 2025, with Amanda Anisimova reaching her maiden Grand Slam final by defeating top seed Aryna Sabalenka 6-4, 4-6, 6-4. The second semi-final was considerably more one-sided, with Iga Swiatek sweeping Belinda Bencic aside 6-2, 6-0 to reach her first final here, having previously won the French Open four times and the US Open once

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Switzerland v Algeria (Kick-off 4.00am). Coverage of the round-of-32 match at BC Place Vancouver in Canada. Switzerland finished top of Group B after they beat co-hosts Canada 2-1 in their final group game thanks to second-half goals from Ruben Vargas and Johan Manzambi. That victory left them on seven points while Canada had to settle for second place on four points, and they face an Algerian side who booked their place with a dramatic 3-3 draw against Austria in their final group match

programme.title: Wimbledon 2026
programme.desc: Coverage of day five of the third Grand Slam tournament of the year, held at the All England Club, featuring men's and ladies' third-round singles matches. Taylor Fritz and Alejandro Davidovich Fokina were first up on Centre Court on the fifth day last year, and it was fifth seed Fritz who took the honours in a 6-4, 6-3, 6-7, 6-1 victory. Carlos Alcaraz followed them, and the 2023 and 2024 champion triumphed 6-1, 3-6, 6-3, 6-4 against Jan-Lennard Struff. Alcaraz went on to reach another final here, but he is not present this year due to a wrist injury

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Australia v Egypt (Kick-off 7.00pm). Coverage of the round-of-32 match at Dallas Stadium in the USA. Australia finished second in Group D behind United States after they picked up four points from their three matches. They did lose 2-0 to the co-hosts but a 2-0 victory over Turkiye and a 0-0 draw against Paraguay meant they progressed to the knockout stages for the second successive World Cup. Egypt came through their group undefeated, beating New Zealand and drawing with Belgium and Iran, with the winners expected to face Argentina in the last 16
